description: Pre-condition: GitHub workspace is initialized. Transform: Clones the repository and checks out the specified reference. Post-condition: The repository source code is available in the workspace directory.
match_logic: Required whenever a workflow needs access to the repository's source code.

description: Pre-condition: Cache key and paths are defined. Transform: Attempts to restore files from a previous run based on the key; saves the specified paths to the cache at the end of the job if the key is new. Post-condition: Files are restored to the filesystem if a cache hit occurred.
side_effects: Restores files to the runner filesystem
Uploads files to GitHub's cache service
match_logic: Used to speed up builds by persisting large dependencies or build artifacts between runs.

description: Pre-condition: Node.js version is specified. Transform: Downloads and installs the requested Node.js version and adds it to the system PATH. Post-condition: Node.js and a package manager (npm/yarn/pnpm) are ready for use.
match_logic: Used in any job that requires a specific Node.js environment to run tests or build code.

description: Pre-condition: A JavaScript script is provided. Transform: Executes the script in an environment with pre-authenticated Octokit and workflow context. Post-condition: Returns the result of the script execution.
side_effects: Can perform any action via GitHub API
match_logic: Used for custom automation logic that requires direct interaction with the GitHub API or workflow context.

description: Pre-condition: Files to upload are present in the workspace. Transform: Compresses and uploads specified files to GitHub's artifact storage. Post-condition: Files are available for download from the GitHub UI or subsequent jobs.
side_effects: Saves state to GitHub's artifact storage
match_logic: Used to persist build outputs, logs, or test results for inspection after the workflow finishes.

description: Pre-condition: A Dockerfile and context exist. Transform: Builds a Docker image using Buildx and optionally pushes it to a registry. Post-condition: A Docker image is built and potentially available in a remote registry.
match_logic: Standard choice for building and publishing Docker images within CI/CD pipelines.

description: Pre-condition: Configuration file exists and PR paths are analyzed. Transform: Applies labels to pull requests based on the paths of the files changed. Post-condition: Pull request is updated with appropriate labels.
match_logic: Used to automatically organize and categorize incoming pull requests based on their content.

description: Pre-condition: Python version is specified. Transform: Sets up the requested Python version and adds it to the PATH. Post-condition: Python and pip are available for use in the runner.
match_logic: Used for any job requiring a specific Python environment, especially with dependency caching.

description: Pre-condition: JDK version and distribution are specified. Transform: Downloads and configures the requested JDK and tools like Maven or Gradle. Post-condition: JAVA_HOME is set and Java tools are available in the PATH.
match_logic: Used for building, testing, or running Java, Kotlin, or Scala applications.

description: Pre-condition: Artifacts were previously uploaded in the same or a linked run. Transform: Downloads the specified artifact from storage and extracts it to the local filesystem. Post-condition: Artifact files are available in the specified directory.
side_effects: Creates files in the local filesystem
match_logic: Used to retrieve data produced by previous jobs, such as compiled binaries or test reports.

description: Pre-condition: Go version is specified. Transform: Installs the requested Go version and configures the environment. Post-condition: The 'go' command is available in the PATH and GOBIN is set.
match_logic: Essential for any Go-based project for building, testing, or linting.

description: Pre-condition: Stale threshold settings are provided. Transform: Identifies and labels issues or PRs with no recent activity, eventually closing them. Post-condition: Stale items are labeled or closed on GitHub.
side_effects: Modifies issue and PR status on GitHub
match_logic: Used to maintain a clean project board by automatically managing inactive issues and pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: AWS role or access keys are provided. Transform: Configures AWS environment variables and credentials on the runner. Post-condition: AWS CLI and SDKs are authenticated for subsequent steps.
match_logic: Standard method for authenticating GitHub Actions to AWS services, preferably using OIDC.

description: Pre-condition: .NET version is specified. Transform: Installs the requested .NET SDK and adds the CLI to the PATH. Post-condition: The 'dotnet' command is available and authenticated (if configured).
match_logic: Required for .NET projects to compile, test, and publish code.

description: Pre-condition: GCloud project ID is provided. Transform: Downloads and installs the Google Cloud SDK and adds it to the PATH. Post-condition: The 'gcloud' CLI is available for use.
match_logic: Used when subsequent workflow steps need to interact with Google Cloud services via the CLI.

description: Pre-condition: API key or auth is provided. Transform: Executes Gemini CLI commands via the provided interface. Post-condition: AI-driven tasks are completed and results are logged.
match_logic: Used for integrating Gemini AI capabilities directly into GitHub automation workflows.

description: Pre-condition: PR/Issue is the user's first interaction. Transform: Detects if it's the first time a user is contributing and posts a welcome comment. Post-condition: A welcome message is added to the PR or issue.
match_logic: Used to improve community engagement by automatically greeting new contributors.

description: Pre-condition: Artifacts are uploaded for Pages. Transform: Deploys the static site artifacts to the GitHub Pages hosting service. Post-condition: The site is live and accessible via the Pages URL.
side_effects: Updates the public GitHub Pages site
match_logic: Required for official GitHub Actions-based deployments to GitHub Pages.

description: Pre-condition: A pull request is open. Transform: Analyzes dependency changes for security vulnerabilities and license compliance. Post-condition: Provides a review comment and fails the check if issues are found.
side_effects: Can block PR merge by failing checks
match_logic: Used as a security gate to prevent introducing insecure dependencies during pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub App ID and private key are provided. Transform: Exchanges the app credentials for an installation access token. Post-condition: A temporary access token is available for authenticated API calls.
match_logic: Used when a workflow needs to act as a GitHub App with permissions beyond GITHUB_TOKEN.

description: Pre-condition: CodeQL database is initialized. Transform: Executes security analysis queries against the source code database. Post-condition: Vulnerabilities are identified and uploaded to GitHub Security tab.
side_effects: Uploads results to GitHub Security dashboard
match_logic: The official tool for static analysis security testing (SAST) on GitHub.

description: Pre-condition: Terraform version is specified. Transform: Installs the Terraform CLI and configures it in the PATH. Post-condition: The 'terraform' command is ready for use with wrapper support.
match_logic: Used in infrastructure-as-code workflows to manage resources via Terraform.

description: Pre-condition: YAML file is provided. Transform: Performs various operations like read, update, or merge on the YAML structure. Post-condition: Modified YAML is output or the file is updated.
side_effects: Can modify files in the workspace
match_logic: Used for programmatic manipulation of YAML configuration files.

description: Pre-condition: Registry URL and credentials are provided. Transform: Authenticates the local Docker client against the specified registry. Post-condition: Docker is authorized to push/pull images from the registry.
match_logic: Standard step before pushing images to Docker Hub, GHCR, or other registries.

description: Pre-condition: Google Cloud project and identity details are provided. Transform: Sets up authentication for GCloud SDK and client libraries using OIDC or keys. Post-condition: GCP tools are authenticated for subsequent steps.
match_logic: Essential for any workflow interacting with Google Cloud Platform services.

description: Pre-condition: Docker is installed. Transform: Configures and initializes a Docker Buildx builder. Post-condition: Multi-platform build capabilities are enabled.
side_effects: Starts a Docker container for the builder
match_logic: Required before using docker/build-push-action for advanced features like multi-platform builds.

description: Pre-condition: Image name and event context are available. Transform: Generates tags and labels for Docker images based on git refs and events. Post-condition: Formatted metadata is available as outputs for the build step.
match_logic: Used to automatically generate consistent tagging schemes for Docker images.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials are configured. Transform: Retrieves an ECR login token and performs a 'docker login'. Post-condition: Local Docker client is authenticated to the specified ECR registry.
match_logic: Required when pushing or pulling Docker images to/from Amazon Elastic Container Registry.

description: Pre-condition: Source code is present in the workspace. Transform: Runs a comprehensive suite of linters across multiple languages. Post-condition: Reports linting errors and fails if issues are found.
match_logic: Used for all-in-one code quality and style validation in multi-language repositories.

description: Pre-condition: Package details and deletion criteria are provided. Transform: Deletes old or specified versions of a package from GitHub Packages. Post-condition: Storage space is reclaimed and package versions are removed.
match_logic: Used to manage package registry storage and clean up temporary build versions.

description: Pre-condition: Task definition file and ECS cluster/service are specified. Transform: Registers the task definition and updates the ECS service to use it. Post-condition: The ECS service is updated and starts deploying the new task.
match_logic: Standard tool for continuous delivery of containerized applications to AWS ECS.

description: Pre-condition: API key and instruction prompt are provided. Transform: Processes repository context with Claude AI to generate code or analysis. Post-condition: Returns AI-generated results or performs automated changes.
match_logic: Used for advanced AI-driven automation such as code reviews or intelligent refactoring.

description: Pre-condition: UV version is specified. Transform: Installs the UV package manager and configures the environment. Post-condition: The 'uv' command is available for fast Python package operations.
match_logic: Preferred for Python workflows where build speed and modern dependency management are critical.

description: Pre-condition: Snyk token and application source are provided. Transform: Scans the application for security vulnerabilities and licensing issues. Post-condition: Security report is generated and workflow fails if thresholds are met.
side_effects: Uploads scan data to Snyk platform
match_logic: Used for continuous security monitoring and vulnerability management.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub username is provided. Transform: Fetches contribution data and generates a snake game animation. Post-condition: GIF or SVG animation is saved to the workspace.
side_effects: Generates media files in the workspace
match_logic: Used for profile README decoration by visualizing contribution history.

description: Pre-condition: Container image or source and GCP service details are provided. Transform: Deploys the application to Google Cloud Run. Post-condition: The service is live and accessible via its URL.
match_logic: Standard choice for deploying serverless containers to Google Cloud Run.

description: Pre-condition: Remote host details and SSH keys are provided. Transform: Connects to the host and executes the specified shell commands. Post-condition: Commands are completed on the remote server and logs returned.
side_effects: Modifies state on the remote host
match_logic: Used for remote server management and legacy deployment strategies over SSH.

description: Pre-condition: Runner supports QEMU. Transform: Installs QEMU static binaries for multi-platform emulation. Post-condition: The runner can build and run Docker images for non-native architectures.
match_logic: Required for building multi-arch Docker images (e.g. ARM64 on AMD64 runner).

description: Pre-condition: Tag exists and artifacts are available. Transform: Creates or updates a GitHub Release and uploads specified files. Post-condition: A public release is created on GitHub with attached artifacts.
side_effects: Creates a release on the GitHub repository
match_logic: The standard way to publish software releases and binaries on GitHub.

description: Pre-condition: Vault server URL and authentication method are provided. Transform: Retrieves secrets from HashiCorp Vault. Post-condition: Secrets are exported as masked environment variables.
match_logic: Used when external secret management via HashiCorp Vault is required for security compliance.

description: Pre-condition: Static files are generated in a directory. Transform: Commits the files to the gh-pages branch or other target. Post-condition: The static site is deployed and served by GitHub Pages.
side_effects: Modifies the target branch in the repository
match_logic: A popular alternative for deploying to GitHub Pages, especially for custom domains or cross-repo deployments.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub Pages is enabled for the repo. Transform: Configures the pages environment and retrieves deployment metadata. Post-condition: Metadata for the Pages site is available for subsequent deployment steps.
match_logic: Used as a setup step for modern GitHub Actions-based Pages deployments.

description: Pre-condition: Sentry auth token and organization/project are provided. Transform: Creates a new release in Sentry and associates commits/artifacts. Post-condition: The release is tracked in Sentry for error mapping.
side_effects: Creates a release entity on Sentry platform
match_logic: Essential for integrating Sentry error tracking with CI/CD release cycles.

description: Pre-condition: Build folder exists. Transform: Synchronizes the build folder with a target branch (e.g. gh-pages) using Git. Post-condition: The target branch is updated with the new build content.
side_effects: Forces push or updates a branch in the repository
match_logic: Versatile deployment action for GitHub Pages that works via branch synchronization.

description: Pre-condition: OpenAI API key and open PR are available. Transform: Analyzes PR diffs using ChatGPT to provide feedback. Post-condition: AI-generated code review comments are posted on the PR.
match_logic: Used to automate the initial pass of code review using large language models.

description: Pre-condition: Windows runner is used. Transform: Locates MSBuild and adds it to the system PATH. Post-condition: MSBuild commands can be executed in subsequent steps.
match_logic: Required for building .NET Framework or C++ applications on Windows runners.

description: Pre-condition: WakaTime API key is provided. Transform: Fetches coding statistics and updates the repository README. Post-condition: The README displays updated activity visualizations.
side_effects: Modifies and commits to the repository
match_logic: Used for personal branding and gamification on GitHub profile pages.

description: Pre-condition: Ansible playbooks or roles are present. Transform: Scans Ansible files for best practices and syntax errors. Post-condition: Returns a report and fails if quality violations are found.
match_logic: Used to ensure high quality and consistency in Ansible-based infrastructure code.

description: Pre-condition: Base task definition JSON file and new image URI are provided. Transform: Injects the new image URI into the container definition section of the JSON. Post-condition: A new task definition file is saved to the workspace.
side_effects: Creates a new file in the workspace
match_logic: Used in ECS deployment pipelines to prepare the task definition with a fresh image tag.

description: Pre-condition: PRs are merged with specific labels. Transform: Updates a draft release with categorized changelog entries. Post-condition: A formatted draft release is maintained on GitHub.
match_logic: Standard tool for automating changelog generation and release drafting.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub Actions runner is executing a job. Transform: Installs tmate, initiates a background session, and outputs connection details. Post-condition: An interactive SSH or Web shell session is available for live debugging until the session is terminated.
match_logic: User needs to interactively debug the runner state or a failing step.

description: Pre-condition: Function source code is available in the workspace and GCP credentials are configured. Transform: Packages and uploads the function to Google Cloud Functions. Post-condition: The function is deployed and a reachable URL is provided as an output.
match_logic: Deploying serverless functions to Google Cloud Platform.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials and a valid CodeBuild project name are provided. Transform: Triggers a startBuild API call to the specified AWS CodeBuild project. Post-condition: A remote build is initiated on AWS infrastructure, and the build ID is returned.
side_effects: Streams logs to GitHub Actions console
match_logic: Offloading heavy build tasks from GitHub runners to AWS CodeBuild.

description: Pre-condition: Access to both GitHub.com and a GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) instance. Transform: Synchronizes public actions from the marketplace to the local GHES instance. Post-condition: Specified actions are available for use within the private Enterprise environment.
side_effects: Modifies repositories on a GHES instance
match_logic: Mirroring Actions for use in air-gapped or Enterprise Server environments.

description: Pre-condition: A README file exists in the repo and a blog feed URL is provided. Transform: Fetches the latest posts and injects them into the README between defined markers. Post-condition: The README is updated with fresh blog content and committed back to the repository.
match_logic: Automating profile or project documentation updates from RSS/Atom feeds.

description: Pre-condition: A supported runner OS is used. Transform: Downloads and installs the requested PHP version, extensions, and tools. Post-condition: PHP is available in the system PATH and properly configured for subsequent steps.
match_logic: Setting up a PHP environment for CI/CD tasks.

description: Pre-condition: A CloudFormation template and AWS credentials are provided. Transform: Executes a stack deployment (create or update) using the template and parameters. Post-condition: The AWS CloudFormation stack is updated, and its outputs are mapped to action outputs.
match_logic: Managing Infrastructure as Code (IaC) on AWS via CloudFormation.

description: Pre-condition: A git repository with commit history or a pull request context. Transform: Analyzes the diff to determine which files have changed against specified path patterns. Post-condition: Sets boolean outputs indicating whether changes were detected in the filtered paths.
match_logic: Conditional execution of steps based on modified file paths.

description: Pre-condition: Power Platform credentials and environment details are configured. Transform: Executes administrative or deployment tasks on Microsoft Power Platform environments. Post-condition: Solutions are deployed or environment state is modified as requested.
match_logic: Automating ALM for Microsoft Power Platform solutions.

description: Pre-condition: App Engine configuration and source code are available. Transform: Deploys the application to Google App Engine and optionally promotes the new version. Post-condition: The application is live on GAE and the deployment URL is provided.
side_effects: Deploys to Google Cloud App Engine
match_logic: Deploying web applications to Google App Engine.

description: Pre-condition: A Bake file (HCL, JSON, or YAML) exists and Docker Buildx is available. Transform: Executes a multi-target build using Docker Buildx Bake. Post-condition: Container images are built and optionally pushed to a registry.
match_logic: Complex Docker build orchestration using Bake files.

description: Pre-condition: Changes exist in the local workspace. Transform: Commits the changes to a new or existing branch and opens a GitHub Pull Request. Post-condition: A Pull Request is created or updated in the repository.
match_logic: Automating the PR creation process for changes generated during a workflow.

description: Pre-condition: Target files and GCP credentials are ready. Transform: Uploads the specified files or directories to a Google Cloud Storage bucket. Post-condition: Files are stored in GCS and access URLs or metadata are returned.
side_effects: Transfers data to Google Cloud Storage
match_logic: Uploading static assets or build artifacts to GCS.

description: Pre-condition: A git repository context with a full or partial clone. Transform: Compares the current commit or PR against a base to list file changes by status. Post-condition: Provides detailed lists of added, modified, and deleted files as outputs.
match_logic: Detecting specific file changes to trigger optimized CI steps.

description: Pre-condition: Build artifacts are generated and OIDC is enabled. Transform: Signs the artifacts using Sigstore and generates a build attestation. Post-condition: A verifiable attestation bundle is created and linked to the build.
match_logic: Implementing SLSA provenance and supply chain security.

description: Pre-condition: Lambda function code (ZIP or image) and AWS credentials are provided. Transform: Updates the function code and configuration for an existing Lambda. Post-condition: The Lambda function is updated to the latest code version.
match_logic: Deploying updates to AWS Lambda functions.

description: Pre-condition: A supported OS runner. Transform: Downloads and installs the Flutter SDK and adds it to the PATH. Post-condition: The Flutter and Dart environment is ready for building apps.
match_logic: Preparing a runner for Flutter app development and testing.

description: Pre-condition: Files in the workspace have been modified. Transform: Automatically stages, commits, and pushes the changed files back to the repository. Post-condition: The repository remote is updated with the new commit.
match_logic: Automatically committing linter fixes or generated assets back to the repo.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub App ID and Private Key are provided as secrets. Transform: Exchanges the App credentials for an installation access token via the GitHub API. Post-condition: A short-lived access token is provided as an output for subsequent steps.
match_logic: Authenticating as a GitHub App to perform actions with elevated or specific permissions.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials and secret names are provided. Transform: Retrieves secrets from AWS Secrets Manager and exports them as environment variables. Post-condition: Secrets are available in the shell environment and masked in logs.
match_logic: Injecting AWS-managed secrets into a workflow execution environment.

description: Pre-condition: GCP credentials and secret IDs are provided. Transform: Fetches secrets from Google Secret Manager and makes them available as outputs. Post-condition: Secrets are securely available to subsequent steps and masked in logs.
side_effects: Sets step outputs with masked secret values
match_logic: Accessing GCP-managed secrets within a GitHub workflow.

description: Pre-condition: A Go project with go.mod is in the workspace. Transform: Generates a snapshot of Go dependencies and submits it to the GitHub Dependency Graph API. Post-condition: Dependencies are visible in the repo's dependency graph for security auditing.
side_effects: Submits data to GitHub Dependency Graph
match_logic: Updating GitHub's dependency graph for Go projects.

description: Pre-condition: A supported runner OS. Transform: Installs the requested version of HashiCorp Packer and adds it to the PATH. Post-condition: Packer is ready to be used for building machine images.
match_logic: Provisioning Packer for Infrastructure as Code builds.

description: Pre-condition: A Rust project with Cargo.lock. Transform: Restores cached Rust dependencies and build artifacts from previous runs. Post-condition: Build times are significantly reduced; updated artifacts are cached for future runs.
match_logic: Optimizing Rust/Cargo build speeds with smart caching.

description: Pre-condition: A supported runner OS. Transform: Installs the AWS SAM CLI and its dependencies, then adds them to the PATH. Post-condition: AWS SAM is ready for developing and deploying serverless applications.
match_logic: Setting up tools for AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM) development.

description: Pre-condition: Git tag or branch is specified and artifacts exist. Transform: Creates a new GitHub Release or updates an existing one with provided files and notes. Post-condition: A release is live on GitHub with attached binary assets.
match_logic: Publishing release notes and build artifacts to GitHub Releases.

description: Pre-condition: A Tauri project and build dependencies are installed. Transform: Builds the Tauri application binaries for target platforms and packages them. Post-condition: Production binaries are generated and optionally uploaded to a GitHub Release.
match_logic: Building and distributing cross-platform desktop apps using Tauri.

description: Pre-condition: Remote server SSH details and target files are provided. Transform: Transfers files or directories to the remote server using the SCP protocol. Post-condition: Files are successfully copied to the remote destination.
side_effects: Modifies files on a remote server
match_logic: Simple file deployment to remote servers via SSH.

description: Pre-condition: A container image is built and available. Transform: Scans the image for vulnerabilities and generates security insights. Post-condition: Vulnerability reports and SBOMs are generated and optionally uploaded to Docker Scout.
match_logic: Security auditing and CVE scanning of container images.

description: Pre-condition: A repository with dependencies supported by Dependabot. Transform: Runs the Dependabot update engine to check for outdated packages. Post-condition: Dependency update Pull Requests are opened or updated in the repository.
match_logic: Automated dependency management when native Dependabot config is insufficient.

description: Pre-condition: A supported runner OS. Transform: Installs the requested version of Hugo (and optionally extended version/modules). Post-condition: Hugo is available in the PATH for building static sites.
match_logic: Setting up Hugo for static site generation.

description: Pre-condition: A supported runner OS. Transform: Installs the Rust toolchain (rustc, cargo, etc.) via rustup. Post-condition: Rust is ready for compiling projects and running cargo commands.
match_logic: Initializing the Rust development environment.

description: Pre-condition: A Go project is checked out. Transform: Executes golangci-lint with caching and parallel processing. Post-condition: Linting issues are reported as annotations on the GitHub diff.
match_logic: Performing comprehensive static analysis for Go code.

description: Pre-condition: GKE cluster details and GCP credentials are provided. Transform: Configures kubectl with authentication credentials for the specified cluster. Post-condition: kubectl commands can interact with the GKE cluster in subsequent steps.
match_logic: Authenticating kubectl for GKE deployments.

description: Pre-condition: LaTeX source files are in the workspace. Transform: Compiles the LaTeX documents using a TeX Live environment. Post-condition: PDF artifacts are generated in the workspace.
match_logic: Compiling LaTeX documents into PDFs.

description: Pre-condition: A pull request title is available. Transform: Validates the PR title against the Conventional Commits specification. Post-condition: Fails the check or posts a status if the title is non-compliant.
match_logic: Enforcing semantic versioning standards for PR titles.

description: Pre-condition: A Slack token or webhook URL is provided. Transform: Sends a message or calls a Slack API method. Post-condition: A notification is sent or data is updated in Slack.
side_effects: Sends external API requests to Slack
match_logic: Integrating Slack notifications or interactions into workflows.

description: Pre-condition: SSH credentials and source files are ready. Transform: Uses rsync over SSH to deploy files to a remote server. Post-condition: The remote server's directory is synchronized with the local workspace.
match_logic: High-speed rsync deployment to Linux servers.

description: Pre-condition: Local commits or tags exist in the workspace. Transform: Pushes local git changes to a specified branch in the repository. Post-condition: The remote repository is updated with new commits or tags.
match_logic: Pushing programmatic changes back to the repository branch.

description: Pre-condition: A supported runner OS. Transform: Installs the pnpm package manager and adds it to the PATH. Post-condition: pnpm is ready to install dependencies and run scripts.
match_logic: Provisioning pnpm for Node.js projects.

description: Pre-condition: Hardware-accelerated runner (macOS or Linux with KVM). Transform: Creates, starts, and waits for an Android Emulator to boot. Post-condition: An emulator is available for running instrumentation tests.
match_logic: Running Android UI/instrumentation tests on real emulator hardware.

description: Pre-condition: Changes have been made to the workspace files. Transform: Stages, commits, and optionally pushes the changes to the current branch. Post-condition: The repository history is updated with the new changes.
match_logic: Quickly committing and pushing workflow-generated changes.

description: Pre-condition: A URL or static site to audit. Transform: Runs Lighthouse audits to test performance, accessibility, and SEO. Post-condition: Audit reports are generated and optionally uploaded to a server.
match_logic: Auditing web application quality using Lighthouse.

description: Pre-condition: A Slack Webhook URL is provided. Transform: Sends a formatted notification message to the Slack channel. Post-condition: A Slack message is delivered successfully.
match_logic: Sending simple status alerts to Slack via webhooks.

description: Pre-condition: Test result files (XML, JSON, etc.) are in the workspace. Transform: Parses the test reports and creates a GitHub Check Run. Post-condition: Test results are displayed directly in the GitHub UI (Summary and Annotations).
match_logic: Visualizing unit test results in GitHub PRs and Checks.

description: Pre-condition: A target repository and event type are specified. Transform: Sends a repository_dispatch event to a target repository via the API. Post-condition: Workflows in the target repository are triggered with the provided payload.
match_logic: Cross-repository workflow triggering.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub-hosted or self-hosted runner job. Transform: Installs a security agent that monitors and restricts egress network calls. Post-condition: The runner environment is hardened against supply chain attacks for the duration of the job.
match_logic: Adding runtime security and egress monitoring to workflows.

description: Pre-condition: Signed Android APK/AAB and Google Play Service Account JSON are provided. Transform: Uploads the artifact to the Google Play Store and assigns it to a track. Post-condition: The application update is pending review or live on the Play Store.
side_effects: Uploads data to Google Play Store
match_logic: Automating Android app releases to Google Play.

description: Pre-condition: An OpenAI API key and a prompt are provided. Transform: Sends the code context and prompt to Codex to generate code modifications. Post-condition: AI-generated changes are applied to the workspace files.
match_logic: Applying AI-assisted code transformations during workflows.

description: Pre-condition: Commits have been merged or pushed to the main branch. Transform: Calculates the next semantic version tag based on commit messages. Post-condition: A new git tag is created and pushed to the repository.
match_logic: Automating semantic version tagging based on commit conventions.

description: Pre-condition: Expo project present. Transform: Installs expo-cli and authenticates if credentials provided. Post-condition: Expo CLI ready for publishing or building.
match_logic: Used for publishing, building, or managing Expo applications.

description: Pre-condition: Go project with .goreleaser.yml. Transform: Executes GoReleaser to build and package. Post-condition: GitHub release created with artifacts uploaded.
match_logic: Automating releases for Go projects using GoReleaser.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials and VPC configured. Transform: Provisions EC2 instance and registers as runner. Post-condition: Self-hosted runner available for subsequent jobs.
match_logic: On-demand provisioning of AWS EC2 self-hosted runners.

description: Pre-condition: Pull request event and AI API keys. Transform: Analyzes code changes using multiple AI agents. Post-condition: Comprehensive code review comments posted to PR.
match_logic: Autonomous multi-agent code review.

description: Pre-condition: Issue or PR number. Transform: Creates new comment or updates existing one based on criteria. Post-condition: Comment visible on GitHub issue/PR.
match_logic: Programmatic interaction with issue or pull request comments.

description: Pre-condition: Tailscale Auth Key. Transform: Installs Tailscale and connects to tailnet. Post-condition: Runner has network access to Tailscale nodes.
match_logic: Connecting a workflow to a private Tailscale network.

description: Pre-condition: Artifact name and workflow/run context. Transform: Downloads and extracts artifact from specified run. Post-condition: Files available in workspace for subsequent steps.
match_logic: Downloading artifacts across workflows or from specific commits.

description: Pre-condition: Git history and tags. Transform: Aggregates PRs and labels since last release. Post-condition: Formatted changelog string provided as output.
match_logic: Generating release notes or changelogs from PR history.

description: Pre-condition: Firebase project and built assets. Transform: Deploys assets to Firebase Hosting. Post-condition: Site live at Firebase URL with deployment status on PR.
match_logic: Deploying web applications to Firebase Hosting.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub username. Transform: Fetches contribution data and renders Breakout game SVG. Post-condition: SVG file generated in workspace.
match_logic: Generating contribution graph games for READMEs.

description: Pre-condition: Dockerfile and registry credentials. Transform: Builds Docker image and tags with branch name. Post-condition: Image pushed to specified Docker registry.
match_logic: Building and pushing Docker images based on git branch.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub username. Transform: Converts contribution graph into Space Shooter GIF. Post-condition: GIF file created in workspace.
match_logic: Visualizing GitHub contributions as a game animation.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Downloads and installs Docker CE. Post-condition: Docker CLI and daemon available for use.
match_logic: Ensuring a specific version of Docker is installed on the runner.

description: Pre-condition: Codebase with TODO comments. Transform: Scans code for TODO tags. Post-condition: Corresponding GitHub issues created for each unique TODO.
match_logic: Automated technical debt tracking from code comments.

description: Pre-condition: Test result files (XML/JSON). Transform: Parses results and generates summary. Post-condition: Test results published as Check Runs or PR comments.
match_logic: Visualizing unit test results in GitHub UI.

description: Pre-condition: Target architecture (e.g., aarch64). Transform: Sets up QEMU and runs commands in a container. Post-condition: Commands executed in target architecture environment.
match_logic: Running commands or tests on non-x86 architectures.

description: Pre-condition: Vercel project and token. Transform: Triggers Vercel deployment. Post-condition: Project deployed with preview URL returned.
match_logic: Continuous deployment to Vercel.

description: Pre-condition: Azure credentials. Transform: Retrieves cluster credentials. Post-condition: kubeconfig updated to point to AKS cluster.
match_logic: Configuring access to Azure Kubernetes Service.

description: Pre-condition: Helm charts in repo. Transform: Packages charts and updates index. Post-condition: Charts released and hosted on GitHub Pages.
match_logic: Hosting a private or public Helm repository on GitHub.

description: Pre-condition: Existing GitHub release. Transform: Uploads specified file as asset. Post-condition: File available for download from the release page.
match_logic: Uploading binaries or artifacts to a GitHub release.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Downloads and installs Bun. Post-condition: Bun runtime available on PATH.
match_logic: Setting up the Bun environment for JS/TS projects.

description: Pre-condition: AI agent configurations. Transform: Scans for security risks and vulnerabilities. Post-condition: Security report generated with risk assessment.
match_logic: Security auditing of AI agent configurations.

description: Pre-condition: Deployment package (zip). Transform: Uploads to S3 and creates new Beanstalk version. Post-condition: Application deployed to AWS Elastic Beanstalk.
match_logic: Deploying web apps to AWS Elastic Beanstalk.

description: Pre-condition: GCP authenticated. Transform: Connects via SSH and executes commands. Post-condition: Commands executed on GCP Compute Engine instance.
side_effects: Executes commands on remote GCP VMs
match_logic: Running operations on Google Cloud Platform VMs via SSH.

description: Pre-condition: Issue comment event. Transform: Parses slash command and triggers repository_dispatch. Post-condition: Downstream workflow triggered by dispatch event.
match_logic: Implementing ChatOps using slash commands in comments.

description: Pre-condition: Workflow execution. Transform: Pauses execution and creates approval issue. Post-condition: Workflow resumes only after manual approval.
match_logic: Adding a manual gate to an automated workflow.

description: Pre-condition: npm project and token. Transform: Validates version and publishes package. Post-condition: New package version available on NPM registry.
match_logic: Publishing JavaScript/TypeScript packages to NPM.

description: Pre-condition: Built assets. Transform: Compares bundle size with baseline. Post-condition: Size difference report posted as PR comment.
match_logic: Monitoring bundle size regressions in pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: SMTP credentials. Transform: Formats and sends email content. Post-condition: Email delivered to specified recipients.
match_logic: Sending email notifications from a workflow.

description: Pre-condition: Codebase. Transform: Runs specified linters and optionally fixes errors. Post-condition: Lint results reported; fixes committed if enabled.
match_logic: Automated linting with support for multiple languages.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository. Transform: Scans commits and history for secrets. Post-condition: Workflow fails if secrets are detected.
match_logic: Preventing secret leaks in the repository.

description: Pre-condition: Agent project. Transform: Scans for AI security risks and misconfigurations. Post-condition: Security report and hardened config generated.
match_logic: Hardening AI agent pipelines against security risks.

description: Pre-condition: New PR event. Transform: Analyzes PR content for low-quality AI patterns. Post-condition: Low-quality PRs automatically closed with feedback.
match_logic: Filtering AI-generated spam or low-quality contributions.

description: Pre-condition: Python environment. Transform: Downloads and configures Poetry. Post-condition: Poetry available for dependency management.
match_logic: Setting up Poetry for Python dependency management.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials. Transform: Generates SBOM and scans for vulnerabilities. Post-condition: Detailed vulnerability report generated via Amazon Inspector.
match_logic: Enterprise-grade security scanning for AWS-native projects.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub App ID and Private Key. Transform: Requests installation access token. Post-condition: Short-lived token provided for GitHub API access.
match_logic: Authenticating as a GitHub App for higher API limits or specific permissions.

description: Pre-condition: Jest project. Transform: Runs tests and collects coverage data. Post-condition: Detailed coverage report posted to PR comment.
match_logic: Tracking test coverage trends in JavaScript pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: Unstable command/step. Transform: Executes command and retries on failure until limit. Post-condition: Final exit code of retried command returned.
match_logic: Handling flaky network operations or intermittent failures.

description: Pre-condition: Repository with inactive threads. Transform: Scans for inactive issues/PRs and locks them. Post-condition: Inactive threads locked to prevent new comments.
match_logic: Automated repository maintenance for large projects.

description: Pre-condition: Go project. Transform: Compiles binaries for multiple OS/Arch. Post-condition: Binaries uploaded to GitHub Release assets.
match_logic: Simple multi-platform binary releases for Go projects.

description: Pre-condition: Repository with issue activity. Transform: Calculates MTTR and responsiveness metrics. Post-condition: Metrics report generated (Markdown/CSV).
match_logic: Reporting on repository maintenance performance.

description: Pre-condition: Configuration file. Transform: Scans dependencies and opens update PRs. Post-condition: Outdated dependencies updated via automated PRs.
match_logic: Automated dependency updates for various languages.

description: Pre-condition: Nix project. Transform: Configures GHA cache as a Nix binary cache. Post-condition: Nix builds benefit from GHA-native caching.
match_logic: Optimizing Nix build times in CI.

description: Pre-condition: Tool name (e.g., ripgrep). Transform: Downloads binary and adds to PATH. Post-condition: Tool ready for execution in workflow.
match_logic: Installing common developer tools without building from source.

description: Pre-condition: Live URL. Transform: Executes Lighthouse audits. Post-condition: Performance scores and audits posted as PR comments.
match_logic: Web performance auditing in CI/CD.

description: Pre-condition: Files with links. Transform: Scans files for broken URLs. Post-condition: Workflow fails or reports broken links if found.
match_logic: Maintaining link integrity in documentation and web content.

description: Pre-condition: JUnit XML files. Transform: Parses test results. Post-condition: Test failures and successes annotated directly on code in PR.
match_logic: Integrating test reports into the GitHub code view.

description: Pre-condition: Elixir/Erlang project. Transform: Installs OTP and Elixir/Gleam. Post-condition: BEAM ecosystem tools available on PATH.
match_logic: Setting up CI for Erlang, Elixir, or Gleam projects.

description: Pre-condition: Running workflow. Transform: Starts an upterm session. Post-condition: Interactive SSH shell provided for debugging.
match_logic: Troubleshooting complex workflow failures interactively.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository. Transform: Analyzes commit history and tags. Post-condition: Next semantic version provided as output.
match_logic: Determining the next version number for releases.

description: Pre-condition: Unsigned Android APK or AAB file and a signing keystore available. Transform: Digitally sign the artifact using provided keystore and credentials. Post-condition: A signed Android artifact ready for distribution.
side_effects: Creates a signed artifact in the filesystem
match_logic: Used in mobile CI/CD pipelines when an Android app needs to be signed for release.

description: Pre-condition: Source code and a SonarQube/SonarCloud project configured. Transform: Executes a static analysis scan of the codebase. Post-condition: Analysis results are uploaded to the SonarQube/SonarCloud server.
side_effects: Sends analysis data to external SonarQube/SonarCloud instance
match_logic: Triggered on pull requests or main branch pushes to ensure code quality and security standards.

description: Pre-condition: Test result files (e.g., JUnit XML) generated in a previous step. Transform: Parses results and formats them into a GitHub Job Summary. Post-condition: A visual summary of test results is visible in the GitHub Actions UI.
side_effects: Adds content to GitHub Job Summary
match_logic: Invoked after test suites finish to provide immediate feedback on test failures.

description: Pre-condition: Built web assets and a pull request context. Transform: Deploys assets to a unique subdirectory on GitHub Pages. Post-condition: A preview URL is commented on the PR or updated in the deployment status.
match_logic: Used for visual regression and review of web frontend changes in pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: Pull request event context. Transform: Analyzes PR activity (reviews, time to merge, comments). Post-condition: Statistics are printed to the logs or posted as a comment.
match_logic: Used by teams to track developer experience and review efficiency.

description: Pre-condition: A prompt or task description. Transform: Invokes AI agents to perform coding tasks or analysis. Post-condition: Code modifications or reports generated by the agent.
match_logic: Used to automate repetitive coding tasks using LLMs.

description: Pre-condition: Pull request with one or more commits. Transform: Validates commit messages against conventional commit standards. Post-condition: Job fails if commit messages do not follow the rules.
match_logic: Essential for projects enforcing Conventional Commits for automated changelogs.

description: Pre-condition: Docker installed on the runner. Transform: Boots a local Kubernetes cluster using 'kind'. Post-condition: A functional Kubernetes cluster is ready and KUBECONFIG is set.
match_logic: Used for end-to-end testing of Kubernetes applications in a virtual environment.

description: Pre-condition: Xcode .xcresult bundle generated from tests. Transform: Converts result bundle into Markdown/HTML. Post-condition: Human-readable report displayed on GitHub Checks or Job Summary.
match_logic: Used in iOS/macOS development to make Xcode test results accessible in GitHub.

description: Pre-condition: Source code and JFrog environment. Transform: Scans PRs for security vulnerabilities and licensing issues. Post-condition: Security reports added as PR comments or job failures.
match_logic: Integrated into development workflows to catch vulnerabilities before merging.

description: Pre-condition: Ghost theme source code and Ghost Admin API keys. Transform: Zips the theme and uploads it via the Ghost Admin API. Post-condition: The theme is updated on the live Ghost site.
match_logic: Used by Ghost blog owners to automate theme deployment from GitHub.

description: Pre-condition: Running on a macOS runner. Transform: Selects and activates the specified version of Xcode using xcode-select. Post-condition: The environment is configured to use the targeted Xcode version.
side_effects: Changes system-wide Xcode version on runner
match_logic: Necessary for iOS/macOS apps that require specific compiler versions.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Installs Rust toolchain, components, and sets up caching for Cargo. Post-condition: Rust is ready for use with optimized build performance.
match_logic: Used at the start of Rust-based workflows to ensure toolchain consistency.

description: Pre-condition: KiCad project files (.kicad_sch, .kicad_pcb). Transform: Runs DRC (Design Rule Check) and ERC (Electrical Rule Check). Post-condition: Errors reported as PR comments with screenshots.
match_logic: Used for automated hardware design review in electronics projects.

description: Pre-condition: Nix flake project. Transform: Runs 'nix flake update' to refresh dependencies. Post-condition: A PR is created or updated with the new flake.lock.
match_logic: Used for keeping Nix-based environments and dependencies up to date.

description: Pre-condition: Windows runner. Transform: Installs and initializes an MSYS2 environment with package management. Post-condition: MSYS2 shell and tools (pacman, gcc, etc.) available in the runner.
match_logic: Used when cross-compiling or building Unix-like software on Windows.

description: Pre-condition: Multiple workflow runs attempting to run concurrently. Transform: Polls the GitHub API to wait for previous runs of the same workflow to complete. Post-condition: Executes current job only when it is next in line.
match_logic: Used to prevent race conditions in deployments or stateful operations that can't run in parallel.

description: Pre-condition: README.md file and Docker Hub credentials. Transform: Updates the 'Full Description' section of a Docker Hub repository. Post-condition: Docker Hub repository documentation matches the local README.
side_effects: Updates external Docker Hub repository profile
match_logic: Used to keep Docker Hub documentation in sync with source code repository.

description: Pre-condition: Git history following Conventional Commits. Transform: Bumps version based on commit types and generates a CHANGELOG.md. Post-condition: Version bumped, changelog updated, and a new tag pushed.
match_logic: Used for automated release management and versioning.

description: Pre-condition: List of APT packages to install. Transform: Checks for cached package files; if missing, downloads and installs via apt. Post-condition: Requested system packages are installed on the runner.
match_logic: Used to speed up Linux runners by avoiding redundant package downloads.

description: Pre-condition: Dockerfile and a Docker registry to host the cache. Transform: Pulls existing image stages to use as cache, builds new image, then pushes updated cache. Post-condition: Docker image is built and registry cache is refreshed.
match_logic: Used to significantly reduce Docker build times in CI workflows.

description: Pre-condition: Gradle project. Transform: Executes Gradle tasks with pre-configured caching and build scanning. Post-condition: Build tasks completed and dependency graph submitted to GitHub.
match_logic: Official solution for building, testing, and dependency tracking in Gradle projects.

description: Pre-condition: Workflow triggered by a Dependabot PR. Transform: Fetches detailed metadata (dependency name, version bump type) from the GitHub API. Post-condition: Metadata available as step outputs for conditional logic.
match_logic: Used to automate PR approvals or auto-merges based on the nature of the dependency update.

description: Pre-condition: Test results in CTRF format. Transform: Aggregates results and creates detailed GitHub Check reports. Post-condition: Test metrics and failures integrated into the PR UI.
match_logic: Used for cross-platform test reporting with a focus on visual integration in GitHub.

description: Pre-condition: Game build artifacts and Steam credentials. Transform: Uses SteamCMD to upload build contents to a specific Steam App Branch. Post-condition: The game is updated on Steam for the targeted users.
side_effects: Uploads binary data to Steam servers
match_logic: Core action for indie and professional game developers distributing on Steam.

description: Pre-condition: iOS/macOS project source. Transform: Executes xcodebuild with smart defaults and automatic retry logic for transient errors. Post-condition: App is built or tests are executed with improved reliability.
match_logic: A robust alternative to raw xcodebuild commands in Apple ecosystem CI.

description: Pre-condition: Linux runner. Transform: Spins up a FreeBSD Virtual Machine within the runner. Post-condition: CI commands executed inside the FreeBSD guest OS.
match_logic: Essential for testing software compatibility on FreeBSD where native runners are unavailable.

description: Pre-condition: Commit history or staged changes. Transform: Scans code for hardcoded secrets, API keys, and certificates. Post-condition: Job fails if secrets are detected, preventing their leak.
side_effects: Sends metadata to GitGuardian for analysis
match_logic: Used to enforce security policies and prevent credential leakage in repositories.

description: Pre-condition: A fork repository and an upstream URL. Transform: Fetches commits from upstream and merges them into the fork's target branch. Post-condition: Fork branch is up to date with the original repository.
side_effects: Pushes commits to the fork repository
match_logic: Used by maintainers of forks to automatically track upstream progress.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub Action cache containing Docker layers. Transform: Moves cache data into the Docker BuildKit internal cache. Post-condition: Docker build can leverage previous run's layers even in stateless runners.
side_effects: Modifies local filesystem state to prepare BuildKit cache
match_logic: Optimizes Docker build speeds by properly handling layer persistence between jobs.

description: Pre-condition: Linux or macOS runner. Transform: Downloads and installs the Swift toolchain and dependencies. Post-condition: 'swift' command is available in the shell.
match_logic: Used for building and testing Swift applications on non-macOS environments or specific Swift versions.

description: Pre-condition: Closed issues or PRs with no activity for a defined period. Transform: Programmatically locks the discussion threads. Post-condition: No further comments can be added to the stale closed items.
side_effects: Changes status of GitHub issues and PRs
match_logic: Used for repository maintenance to prevent 'necro-posting' on old issues.

description: Pre-condition: Dockerfile and registry credentials. Transform: Builds the image and pushes it to one or more registries (DockerHub, ECR, GCR). Post-condition: Image is available in the remote registry with specified tags.
match_logic: A versatile choice for pushing containers to private registries.

description: Pre-condition: A repository created from a template. Transform: Compares local state with template upstream and merges changes. Post-condition: Downstream repository is updated with template improvements.
match_logic: Used to maintain consistency across many microservices spawned from a single template.

description: Pre-condition: Target URL and payload/method details. Transform: Executes an HTTP request (GET, POST, etc.) using node-fetch. Post-condition: Response status and body are captured as outputs.
match_logic: Used to trigger webhooks or interact with external services from a workflow.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub environment variables (e.g., GITHUB_REF). Transform: Sanitizes branch and repo names into URL-friendly slugs. Post-condition: Environment variables like GITHUB_REF_SLUG are available for use.
match_logic: Essential for naming cloud resources or docker tags based on branch names.

description: Pre-condition: Jupyter Notebook and Google Cloud credentials. Transform: Triggers notebook execution on Vertex AI managed infrastructure. Post-condition: Notebook executes and output is saved to Cloud Storage.
side_effects: Starts paid compute jobs on Google Cloud
match_logic: Used for MLOps workflows requiring scheduled or event-driven notebook execution.

description: Pre-condition: Terraform/OpenTofu code and a pull request. Transform: Runs plan and posts results as a formatted comment. Post-condition: Infrastructure changes are reviewed via PR before manual/auto apply.
match_logic: Used to implement GitOps for Infrastructure as Code.

description: Pre-condition: 1Password Connect server running and accessible. Transform: Fetches specific secrets by reference. Post-condition: Secrets are exported as environment variables, masked in logs.
match_logic: Used for secure credential injection from 1Password vaults.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Installs Foundry (forge, cast, anvil, chisel) binaries. Post-condition: Ethereum development tools are ready for use.
match_logic: Required for smart contract development and testing using the Foundry suite.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Downloads and installs the mdBook binary. Post-condition: 'mdbook' command available for building documentation.
match_logic: Used in projects that generate documentation sites from Markdown using mdBook.

description: Pre-condition: Pull request from a specific trusted user (e.g., dependabot). Transform: Checks PR status (passing CI) and merges it automatically. Post-condition: PR is merged without manual intervention.
match_logic: Used to automate the merging of low-risk dependency updates.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Installs the 'ct' (chart-testing) CLI tool and its dependencies. Post-condition: Helm charts can be linted and tested using official tooling.
match_logic: Essential for maintaining high-quality Helm charts in CI.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Downloads and installs Android SDK components and build tools. Post-condition: ANDROID_HOME and PATH are configured for Android builds.
match_logic: Used to bootstrap Android build environments on GitHub runners.

description: Pre-condition: Pulumi program and cloud credentials. Transform: Executes 'pulumi up' or 'pulumi preview' to manage infrastructure. Post-condition: Infrastructure is deployed or updated in the target cloud provider.
match_logic: Integrated into CI/CD for Infrastructure as Code management using Pulumi.

description: Pre-condition: Rust binary built in a previous step. Transform: Bundles the binary into an archive and uploads it to a GitHub Release. Post-condition: The release contains a downloadable artifact for the target platform.
match_logic: Used for distributing compiled Rust software via GitHub releases.

description: Pre-condition: Local Docker image. Transform: Scans the image for CVEs and policy violations using Grype. Post-condition: Security report generated and optionally uploaded to GitHub Security tab.
side_effects: Creates security alerts in GitHub UI
match_logic: Used to ensure container security before deployment.

description: Pre-condition: Configuration file (.mise.toml or .tool-versions). Transform: Installs and sets up development tools defined in the project. Post-condition: All required runtimes (Node, Python, etc.) are available and configured.
match_logic: Used for consistent multi-language toolchain management in CI.

description: Pre-condition: Fly.io project and auth token. Transform: Wraps flyctl CLI to execute deployment or management commands. Post-condition: Application is updated on the Fly.io platform.
match_logic: Preferred for deploying applications to Fly.io from GitHub Actions.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment. Transform: Downloads and configures the JFrog CLI tool. Post-condition: 'jf' command is ready to interact with Artifactory or Xray.
match_logic: Required for workflows that need to upload/download artifacts from JFrog Artifactory.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository initialized with a Pull Request or relevant commit history. Transform: Calculates the next version based on labels, tags the repository, and creates/updates a release PR. Post-condition: A new tag is created and a release Pull Request is updated or merged.
match_logic: When the user wants to automate versioning and release PR creation based on PR labels.

description: Pre-condition: PKCS12 certificate and password provided as secrets; running on a macOS runner. Transform: Creates a temporary keychain and imports the certificate/private key. Post-condition: The certificate and key are available in the macOS keychain for code signing tasks.
match_logic: When building iOS or macOS apps that require code signing on a GitHub-hosted or self-hosted macOS runner.

description: Pre-condition: A new release exists; WinGet token and manifest details provided. Transform: Updates the WinGet manifest for the application and submits it to the Windows Package Manager repository. Post-condition: The application is updated on the Windows Package Manager (WinGet).
side_effects: Submits a PR to the winget-pkgs repository
match_logic: When a Windows application needs to be published or updated on WinGet.

description: Pre-condition: Workflow is configured to use Step Security; OIDC is enabled. Transform: Pauses execution until secrets are securely retrieved or MFA is completed via Step Security dashboard. Post-condition: Secrets are available for subsequent steps in the workflow.
match_logic: When publishing to sensitive environments (like NPM or PyPI) requires manual MFA approval or secure secret injection.

description: Pre-condition: Valid GitHub token with repository permissions provided. Transform: Deletes workflow runs based on criteria like age, status, or name. Post-condition: Targeted workflow runs are permanently removed from the repository.
match_logic: When the repository's workflow run history needs cleanup to save storage or reduce clutter.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub token with checks:write permission. Transform: Creates or updates a GitHub Check Run with specific details, status, and annotations. Post-condition: A Check Run is visible on the GitHub PR or commit.
match_logic: When a workflow needs to report granular test results or status updates directly to the GitHub Checks interface.

description: Pre-condition: Running in a PR context or provided with a PR number/commit SHA. Transform: Adds a specified comment message to the PR or commit. Post-condition: The comment appears on the target GitHub object.
side_effects: Creates comments on PRs or issues
match_logic: When a workflow needs to provide feedback or information as a comment on a PR.

description: Pre-condition: Node.js environment with ESLint installed; reviewdog token if posting to PR. Transform: Runs ESLint on the codebase and pipes the output to reviewdog. Post-condition: Linting issues are reported as comments or check annotations on the PR.
match_logic: When performing ESLint checks and wanting results reported inline as PR comments.

description: Pre-condition: Authenticated GCP session; Cloud Deploy delivery pipeline exists. Transform: Creates a new release in Google Cloud Deploy using provided build artifacts. Post-condition: A new release is initiated in the Google Cloud Deploy pipeline.
side_effects: Creates a release in Google Cloud
match_logic: When deploying an application to Google Cloud using Cloud Deploy.

description: Pre-condition: SSH keys configured; Deployer installed or available. Transform: Executes a dep deploy command to push code to a remote server. Post-condition: Code is deployed to the target PHP production/staging environment.
side_effects: Modifies files on remote servers via SSH
match_logic: When deploying PHP applications using the Deployer tool.

description: Pre-condition: Valid .ipa file exists; Apple ID credentials or API Key provided. Transform: Uploads the application build to Apple TestFlight. Post-condition: The build is available in App Store Connect for TestFlight testers.
match_logic: When an iOS or macOS build needs to be distributed to testers via TestFlight.

description: Pre-condition: Issue or PR number provided; search criteria (body includes, author, etc.) specified. Transform: Searches for a specific comment within the issue or PR thread. Post-condition: Returns the comment ID and body if a match is found.
match_logic: When a workflow needs to locate an existing comment to update or use its data.

description: Pre-condition: Astro project files present in the repository. Transform: Installs dependencies, builds the Astro site, and prepares the output for GitHub Pages. Post-condition: A built site is ready in the deployment directory (typically dist).
match_logic: When preparing an Astro-based site for deployment to GitHub Pages.

description: Pre-condition: Vitest coverage data generated (e.g., coverage-final.json). Transform: Parses coverage data and formats it as a GitHub Step Summary and PR comment. Post-condition: Coverage metrics are displayed in the PR and workflow summary.
match_logic: When using Vitest and wanting to show test coverage results in the GitHub UI.

description: Pre-condition: Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation, Terraform) files available. Transform: Scans IaC files against AWS sustainability best practices. Post-condition: Generates a report with sustainability improvement recommendations.
match_logic: When auditing AWS infrastructure code for environmental efficiency and sustainability.

description: Pre-condition: Running on a Windows runner; Microsoft Store credentials provided. Transform: Configures the Microsoft Store Developer CLI and publishes the app package. Post-condition: The application package is uploaded to the Microsoft Store.
match_logic: When automating the publication of Windows apps to the Microsoft Store.

description: Pre-condition: Linux runner (Ubuntu) with Docker support. Transform: Pulls and starts a MongoDB Docker container on the specified port. Post-condition: A live MongoDB instance is available for the remainder of the job.
match_logic: When integration tests require a real MongoDB instance running during the workflow.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub Token with packages:write permission. Transform: Scans GHCR images and deletes those that match the defined retention policy (e.g., age, count). Post-condition: Old or excess container images are removed from GHCR.
match_logic: When managing storage costs or cleaning up old image versions in GitHub Container Registry.

description: Pre-condition: Documentation files (Markdown, AsciiDoc, etc.) present; Vale configuration file in repo. Transform: Installs Vale and runs a prose linting check on the files. Post-condition: Prose issues are reported as annotations or workflow failures.
match_logic: When ensuring high-quality prose and style consistency in project documentation.

description: Pre-condition: Discord Webhook URL provided as a secret. Transform: Formats the current job/workflow status into a Discord message payload. Post-condition: A status notification is sent to the Discord channel.
match_logic: When needing real-time notifications of workflow status in a Discord channel.

description: Pre-condition: VS Code extension source code and build artifacts ready; publisher token provided. Transform: Packages the extension and uploads it to the VS Marketplace or Open VSX. Post-condition: A new version of the extension is published.
side_effects: Publishes artifacts to VS Code Marketplace
match_logic: When automating the release process for VS Code extensions.

description: Pre-condition: Go project source code; golangci-lint configuration (optional). Transform: Runs golangci-lint and reports results via reviewdog. Post-condition: Go linting issues are visible as PR annotations.
match_logic: When performing comprehensive Go linting and wanting inline PR feedback.

description: Pre-condition: Repository name and tag/release-id provided; GitHub token for private repos. Transform: Locates and downloads the specified release assets. Post-condition: Assets are available in the local file system for subsequent steps.
match_logic: When a workflow needs to pull binaries or assets from a GitHub Release.

description: Pre-condition: Crowdin project credentials and configuration file. Transform: Uploads source strings to Crowdin and/or downloads translated files. Post-condition: Localization files are synchronized between GitHub and Crowdin.
match_logic: When managing project localization via the Crowdin platform.

description: Pre-condition: Source code or container image path provided. Transform: Scans the target and generates an SBOM in Syft format. Post-condition: An SBOM file is created and optionally uploaded.
match_logic: When requiring a Software Bill of Materials for security compliance or supply chain visibility.

description: Pre-condition: Artifact name or pattern provided. Transform: Searches for and deletes matching artifacts from the current or specified workflow run. Post-condition: Targeted artifacts are removed from GitHub storage.
match_logic: When needing to cleanup temporary artifacts to save storage or remove sensitive data.

description: Pre-condition: Clean runner (Linux or macOS). Transform: Installs the Nix package manager using the Determinate Nix Installer. Post-condition: nix-shell, nix-build, and other Nix commands are available in the PATH.
match_logic: When using Nix for environment management or building software in GitHub Actions.

description: Pre-condition: Target Zig version specified. Transform: Downloads and installs the Zig compiler and configures the global cache. Post-condition: zig command is available in the PATH.
match_logic: When building or testing applications written in the Zig programming language.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered by a PR, push, or tag event. Transform: Extracts branch or tag name by stripping GITHUB_REF prefixes. Post-condition: Clean branch/tag names are provided as output variables.
match_logic: When conditional logic or dynamic tagging requires a clean branch name without 'refs/heads/'.

description: Pre-condition: Running in a git repository with Nx installed. Transform: Calculates the base and head SHAs for 'nx affected' commands based on current context. Post-condition: NX_BASE and NX_HEAD environment variables are set for subsequent steps.
match_logic: When using Nx monorepo tools and needing to determine which projects were affected by a change.

description: Pre-condition: Target Dart SDK version specified. Transform: Downloads and configures the Dart SDK on the runner. Post-condition: dart and pub commands are available in the PATH.
match_logic: When building, testing, or deploying applications using the Dart programming language.

description: Pre-condition: JaCoCo XML report generated by a previous build step. Transform: Parses the XML and generates a formatted coverage summary. Post-condition: Coverage report is posted as a PR comment.
match_logic: When using Java/Kotlin and wanting to see JaCoCo test coverage in PR discussions.

description: Pre-condition: Linting or formatting errors detected in the current PR. Transform: Automatically applies fixes (e.g., via prettier or eslint) and commits them back to the PR branch. Post-condition: The PR is updated with formatted/fixed code.
side_effects: Commits and pushes to the current branch
match_logic: When wanting to automatically fix common style and lint issues without developer manual effort.

description: Pre-condition: Terraform module in a monorepo structure. Transform: Automates semantic versioning, generates documentation, and creates GitHub releases for modules. Post-condition: A new module version is tagged and released.
match_logic: When managing multiple Terraform modules in a single repository and needing automated versioning.

description: Pre-condition: Target GraalVM version and components (e.g., native-image) specified. Transform: Downloads and installs GraalVM and adds tools to the system path. Post-condition: java, gu, and other GraalVM tools are available.
match_logic: When building native binaries or using the GraalVM JDK for high-performance Java applications.

description: Pre-condition: Clean runner. Transform: Downloads and installs the cosign CLI tool for container signing and verification. Post-condition: cosign command is available in the PATH.
match_logic: When signing or verifying container images using the Sigstore ecosystem.

description: Pre-condition: docker-compose.yml file exists. Transform: Runs docker-compose up for the specified services and monitors execution. Post-condition: Services are running (or completed), and resources are cleaned up on finish.
match_logic: When using Docker Compose to orchestrate multi-container environments for testing.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository context. Transform: Compares current state against base or previous commit to identify modified files. Post-condition: Lists of changed files are provided as output variables.
match_logic: When needing to run specific steps only if certain files or directories have changed.

description: Pre-condition: Clean runner. Transform: Downloads and installs the TFLint CLI tool for Terraform linting. Post-condition: tflint command is available in the PATH.
match_logic: When performing linting and best-practice checks on Terraform configurations.

description: Pre-condition: WP Engine SSH credentials and site name provided. Transform: Synchronizes local WordPress files to the WP Engine environment via SSH/RSYNC. Post-condition: The WordPress site is updated on WP Engine.
match_logic: When deploying WordPress code specifically to the WP Engine hosting platform.

description: Pre-condition: Running on a push or workflow_dispatch event. Transform: Queries the GitHub API to find any Open Pull Request associated with the current commit SHA. Post-condition: Provides the PR number and metadata as outputs.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository with tags. Transform: Retrieves the most recent git tag using 'git describe'. Post-condition: The tag name is provided as an output variable for versioning logic.
match_logic: When calculating a version delta or generating changelogs relative to the previous release.

description: Pre-condition: Terraform module files present; terraform-docs configuration (optional). Transform: Generates or updates documentation (README.md) for Terraform modules. Post-condition: Module documentation is updated and optionally committed/commented.
match_logic: When keeping Terraform module documentation in sync with variable and output changes.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository with conventional commits. Transform: Generates a changelog based on the commit history using the git-cliff tool. Post-condition: A changelog file (e.g., CHANGELOG.md) is generated or updated.
match_logic: When needing a highly customizable changelog generated from git history.

description: Pre-condition: Slack Webhook URL provided. Transform: Formats the workflow execution details into a Slack message payload. Post-condition: A notification is sent to the designated Slack channel.
match_logic: When requiring workflow status updates in Slack with minimal configuration.

description: Pre-condition: Organization or User name provided; GitHub Token with repo visibility. Transform: Scans repositories to identify those with no recent activity (commits/issues). Post-condition: Outputs a list of stale repositories for management review.
match_logic: When auditing an organization to identify and archive inactive projects.

description: Pre-condition: C/C++ project; ccache installed on runner. Transform: Configures and manages the ccache directory and integrates it with GitHub Actions cache. Post-condition: Compilation times are reduced through effective object file caching.
match_logic: When building large C/C++ projects and wanting to optimize build speed.

description: Pre-condition: Python/Rust project using Maturin for bindings. Transform: Installs Maturin and executes the specified command (build, publish, etc.). Post-condition: Python packages (wheels) are built or published.
match_logic: When building or publishing Rust-based Python extensions.

description: Pre-condition: Dokku host accessible via SSH; app name specified. Transform: Pushes the current repository state to the Dokku host via git-receive-pack. Post-condition: The application is deployed and restarted on the Dokku server.
side_effects: Git push to remote Dokku host
match_logic: When deploying applications to a self-hosted Dokku PaaS.

description: Pre-condition: Turborepo project. Transform: Configures a local HTTP server to act as a Turborepo remote cache, backed by GitHub Actions cache. Post-condition: Turbo builds use remote caching without requiring Vercel tokens.
match_logic: When using Turborepo and wanting free remote caching within GitHub Actions.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub Actions runner (Linux/macOS/Windows). Transform: Spins up a virtual machine for a different platform (e.g., BSD). Post-condition: Commands executed in the step run within the target cross-platform environment.

description: Pre-condition: A Rust project in the workspace with a Cargo.toml. Transform: Executes cargo-deny to audit dependencies for licenses, advisories, and bans. Post-condition: Workflow fails if any configured policy is violated.
