description: Pre-condition: An open Pull Request created by Dependabot. Transform: Validates PR requirements (e.g., CI passing) and issues an approval and merge command. Post-condition: The Dependabot PR is merged and the branch is deleted.
match_logic: Use to automate the tedious process of approving and merging safe Dependabot updates.

description: Pre-condition: Standard GitHub context variables available. Transform: Derives clean, short, and useful environment variables (e.g., CI_REF_NAME). Post-condition: Additional environment variables are exported for subsequent workflow steps.
side_effects: Exports multiple environment variables to GITHUB_ENV
match_logic: Use when you need standardized, easy-to-use environment variables like branch names or PR numbers without manual parsing.

description: Pre-condition: A file containing issue content exists in the workspace. Transform: Reads the file and creates a new GitHub issue with the file content as the body. Post-condition: A new issue is created in the repository.
side_effects: Creates a new issue in the GitHub repository
match_logic: Use to automatically report results, reports, or logs from a workflow as a persistent GitHub issue.

description: Pre-condition: Allure results directory exists in the workspace. Transform: Processes results and generates a static HTML Allure report. Post-condition: Report files are ready for hosting or deployment.
side_effects: Creates report files in the workspace
match_logic: Use to transform raw Allure XML/JSON test results into a human-readable HTML dashboard.

description: Pre-condition: Repository with issues/PRs and defined staleness thresholds. Transform: Scans issues/PRs for inactivity and adds labels or closes them based on configuration. Post-condition: Stale issues are marked or closed to maintain project hygiene.
match_logic: Use to automatically manage and close inactive issues and pull requests in a repository.

description: Pre-condition: Markdown files exist in the workspace. Transform: Runs markdownlint-cli2 against specified patterns. Post-condition: Workflow fails if linting errors are found in Markdown files.
match_logic: Use to enforce styling and formatting consistency in Markdown documentation files.

description: Pre-condition: Git history with conventional commits. Transform: Extracts commit messages and generates a formatted changelog entry based on tags. Post-condition: A changelog file is updated or a changelog string is provided.
side_effects: Modifies files in the workspace (if configured)

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment without specific OpenTofu version. Transform: Downloads and installs a specific version of OpenTofu CLI. Post-condition: 'tofu' command is available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use when you need to run OpenTofu (IaC) commands in your workflow.

description: Pre-condition: A Dart/Flutter project in the workspace. Transform: Runs 'dart analyze' and formats output as GitHub annotations. Post-condition: Analysis results are visible as inline annotations in PRs.

description: Pre-condition: A Vuepress project in the workspace. Transform: Builds the Vuepress site and pushes the static assets to a target branch. Post-condition: The site is updated on GitHub Pages.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment needs specific LLVM/Clang versions. Transform: Downloads and installs the requested LLVM/Clang toolchain. Post-condition: LLVM binaries and headers are available in the system.

description: Pre-condition: A Pull Request with diffs. Transform: Analyzes code changes using AI models to identify high-risk areas. Post-condition: Adds comments or labels to PRs flagging potential risks.

description: Pre-condition: An API endpoint and optional credentials. Transform: Fetches data from the API and saves it as a file in the workspace. Post-condition: Data is available for subsequent steps.

description: Pre-condition: Repository exists. Transform: Creates a new git branch from a specified SHA or branch. Post-condition: A new branch exists on the remote repository.

match_logic: Use when you need to run the standard test suite for a Julia language package.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Downloads and installs Nushell. Post-condition: 'nu' command is available in PATH.
match_logic: Use when you need to execute Nushell scripts or use the 'nu' command in subsequent steps.

description: Pre-condition: A Go project requiring CGO cross-compilation. Transform: Runs xgo in a docker container. Post-condition: Cross-compiled binaries are generated.
match_logic: Use when compiling a Go application with C dependencies (CGO) for multiple target platforms.

description: Pre-condition: A repository containing Lua source code. Transform: Executes StyLua to check or format code. Post-condition: Lua files are formatted, or workflow fails if formatting issues are found.
side_effects: May modify Lua files if configured to format
match_logic: Use when you need to lint or format Lua code using StyLua.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Installs and starts a MySQL or MariaDB server. Post-condition: Database server is running and accessible on a local port.
match_logic: Use when your tests require a live MySQL or MariaDB database instance.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered by an issue_comment event. Transform: Parses the comment body for slash commands. Post-condition: Outputs the parsed command and arguments if found.
match_logic: Use when you want to build ChatOps workflows triggered by /commands in issue or PR comments.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered on release or tag creation. Transform: Moves the specified tag to the current commit. Post-condition: The repository has an updated moving tag.
side_effects: Updates git tags on the remote
match_logic: Use when you want to maintain a floating tag like `v1` or `latest` that points to the most recent release.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Downloads and installs Free Pascal Compiler and Lazarus IDE. Post-condition: 'fpc' and 'lazbuild' are available in PATH.
match_logic: Use when you need to build Pascal projects using the Lazarus IDE or Free Pascal Compiler.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Installs the 'rye' Python project management tool and configures caching. Post-condition: 'rye' is available in PATH.
match_logic: Use when your Python project uses Rye for dependency management and building.

description: Pre-condition: Running on Linux or macOS. Transform: Configures and starts the pre-installed PostgreSQL server. Post-condition: PostgreSQL is running and ready for connections.
match_logic: Use when you need a local PostgreSQL database for testing, leveraging the pre-installed binaries on GitHub runners.

description: Pre-condition: A valid semver string is provided. Transform: Calculates the next major, minor, and patch versions. Post-condition: Provides the calculated versions as outputs.
match_logic: Use when you need to determine the next logical semantic versions based on a current version string.

description: Pre-condition: Variables provided to the action. Transform: Serializes inputs into a JSON payload. Post-condition: A JSON file is written to the runner.
match_logic: Use when you need to construct a JSON metadata file from workflow variables.

description: Pre-condition: Binaries and an nfpm configuration file exist. Transform: Runs 'nfpm' to generate Linux packages. Post-condition: Package files are created.
match_logic: Use when you need to create OS-native packages (deb, rpm, apk) using nfpm.

description: Pre-condition: A repository with Dockerfiles, triggered on a PR. Transform: Runs 'hadolint' and pipes output to 'reviewdog'. Post-condition: Linting errors are posted as PR comments or annotations.
match_logic: Use when you want to lint Dockerfiles and have the results annotated directly on the Pull Request.

description: Pre-condition: A repository with CSS/SCSS files, triggered on a PR. Transform: Runs 'stylelint' and pipes output to 'reviewdog'. Post-condition: Linting errors are posted as PR comments or annotations.
match_logic: Use when you want to lint stylesheets using stylelint and have results annotated on the Pull Request.

description: Pre-condition: A Rust project. Transform: Installs cross-compilation toolchains. Post-condition: Rust is ready to build/test for non-native architectures.
match_logic: Use when you need to build or test Rust code for architectures different from the runner host.

description: Pre-condition: An unsigned APK or AAB exists, and keystore secrets are available. Transform: Uses 'apksigner' and/or 'jarsigner' to sign the app. Post-condition: A signed APK/AAB is generated.
match_logic: Use when you need to cryptographically sign an Android APK or AAB before distribution.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered by a release event, Discord webhook URL provided. Transform: Formats the release data into a Discord embed. Post-condition: A message is posted to Discord.
match_logic: Use when you want to notify a Discord channel automatically whenever a new GitHub Release is published.

description: Pre-condition: A repository with GitHub Actions workflows. Transform: Scans workflows to verify 'uses:' statements use full SHAs. Post-condition: Fails if non-SHA versions are found.
match_logic: Use when enforcing strict security policies that require all actions to be pinned by commit hash.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Downloads and installs the 'scw' binary, configures authentication. Post-condition: 'scw' command is available and authenticated.
match_logic: Use when you need to interact with Scaleway cloud resources via the CLI.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Clones the repository using pure git commands or shell. Post-condition: Repository code is available in the workspace.
match_logic: Use as a Node.js-free alternative to actions/checkout, particularly useful in environments where Node is unwanted.

description: Pre-condition: A Go project with generated coverage profiles. Transform: Runs 'goveralls' to upload reports to Coveralls.io. Post-condition: Coverage is reported to Coveralls.
match_logic: Use when you want to send Go test coverage profiles to Coveralls.io.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered by an issue comment. Transform: Reads the comment to see if the user requested specific labels. Post-condition: Applies or removes labels on the issue.
match_logic: Use when you want to allow non-maintainers to label their own issues based on their comments.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered on a PR. Transform: Runs LanguageTool on text/markdown files and formats output via reviewdog. Post-condition: Grammar/spelling errors are annotated on the PR.
match_logic: Use when you want automated grammar and spell checking on text/markdown files in Pull Requests.

description: Pre-condition: A PR is open. Transform: Calls the GitHub API to change PR state to closed. Post-condition: The PR is closed.
match_logic: Use when you need to automatically close a pull request conditionally.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Executes an inline script or file using 'pwsh' (PowerShell Core). Post-condition: Script completes, variables can be passed.
match_logic: Use when you need to run PowerShell scripts with access to GitHub Actions context variables as cmdlets.

description: Pre-condition: An input string is provided. Transform: Modifies the case of the string. Post-condition: Returns the formatted string.
match_logic: Use when you need to normalize string casing (e.g., converting a branch name to lowercase for use in a URL).

description: Pre-condition: An Ansible collection repository. Transform: Runs various linting and testing tools specific to Ansible. Post-condition: Test results are generated.
match_logic: Use when testing and verifying Ansible content collections.

description: Pre-condition: Docker is available. Transform: Installs DDEV and its dependencies. Post-condition: 'ddev' command is available.
match_logic: Use when your project uses DDEV for local development and you want to use it in CI.

description: Pre-condition: Input text and an API key for a translation provider. Transform: Calls the translation API. Post-condition: Returns the translated text.
match_logic: Use when you need to translate text (e.g., release notes, comments) automatically.

description: Pre-condition: Apple software binaries and signing credentials exist. Transform: Uses 'rcodesign' to sign and optionally submit for notarization. Post-condition: Binaries are signed/notarized.
match_logic: Use when you need to sign and notarize macOS/iOS applications, particularly from non-macOS runners.

description: Pre-condition: Running on Windows. Transform: Creates and mounts an ReFS Dev Drive. Post-condition: Dev Drive is available for faster I/O.
match_logic: Use when you need faster disk I/O on Windows runners, leveraging the Dev Drive feature.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Retrieves the current system time and formats it. Post-condition: The formatted time string is outputted.
match_logic: Use when you need the current date or time formatted for use in tags, names, or messages.

description: Pre-condition: A built Gatsby site and AWS credentials. Transform: Uploads the 'public' folder to S3 and optionally invalidates CloudFront. Post-condition: Site is deployed.
match_logic: Use when deploying a static Gatsby website to AWS S3.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub App token or PAT with org read access. Transform: Queries the GitHub API for team memberships of a user. Post-condition: Returns the list of teams.
match_logic: Use when you need to authorize actions based on a user's membership in GitHub Teams.

description: Pre-condition: A repository with '.github/workflows'. Transform: Runs the 'actionlint' binary to parse and validate workflows. Post-condition: Lint errors are outputted.
match_logic: Use when you want to statically analyze GitHub Actions workflow files for syntax errors and common mistakes.

description: Pre-condition: A repository with JSON/YAML files. Transform: Parses the files to ensure they are well-formed. Post-condition: Fails if any files are invalid.
match_logic: Use when you want to ensure all JSON and YAML files in a repository are syntactically valid.

description: Pre-condition: A 'Dangerfile' and a triggered PR. Transform: Executes Danger to evaluate PR rules. Post-condition: Danger leaves comments or fails the PR based on rules.
match_logic: Use when you want to automate code review chores using Danger (e.g., checking PR size, missing changelog).

description: Pre-condition: Markdown files with links. Transform: Runs 'linkinator' to crawl links. Post-condition: Fails if dead links are found.
side_effects: Sends HTTP requests to external sites
match_logic: Use when you want to continuously check for broken links in your documentation or markdown files.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Installs and starts a Redis server. Post-condition: Redis is running and accessible on localhost.
match_logic: Use when your tests require a running Redis instance.

description: Pre-condition: KVM/VirtualBox available on runner. Transform: Boots a Solaris Virtual Machine. Post-condition: CI commands are executed inside the VM.
match_logic: Use when you need to test code specifically on the Solaris operating system.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Downloads and configures the LunarG Vulkan SDK. Post-condition: Vulkan headers and tools are available.
match_logic: Use when building graphics applications that depend on the Vulkan SDK.

description: Pre-condition: A git repository. Transform: Parses git commit data using shell commands. Post-condition: Commit attributes are set as environment variables.
match_logic: Use when you need easy access to git commit metadata without writing custom shell scripts.

description: Pre-condition: Running on Windows. Transform: Downloads and installs Cygwin and specified packages. Post-condition: Cygwin environment is available.
match_logic: Use when you need POSIX compatibility or specific Linux tools on a Windows runner via Cygwin.

description: Pre-condition: A PR is opened or updated. Transform: Evaluates rules against PR metadata. Post-condition: Labels are applied.
match_logic: Use when you want a highly configurable automated labeling system based on PR attributes.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Installs the Scala Build Tool ('sbt'). Post-condition: 'sbt' command is available.
match_logic: Use when building Scala or Java projects managed by sbt.

description: Pre-condition: Linux/Windows/macOS runner. Transform: Downloads and installs specified version of yt-dlp. Post-condition: `yt-dlp` is available in PATH.
match_logic: When the workflow needs to download media using yt-dlp.

description: Pre-condition: Issue exists. Transform: Closes the issue using GitHub API. Post-condition: Issue status is set to closed.
match_logic: When an issue needs to be closed automatically based on workflow logic.

description: Pre-condition: URL is accessible. Transform: Downloads content from URL and saves to target directory. Post-condition: File exists at target location.
match_logic: When a file needs to be downloaded from an external URL during the workflow.

description: Pre-condition: Pull Request event. Transform: Validates PR title against a regex pattern. Post-condition: Fails if title doesn't match regex.
match_logic: When enforcing naming conventions for Pull Request titles.

description: Pre-condition: Executable file exists. Transform: Runs UPX compression on the file. Post-condition: Executable is smaller and compressed.
match_logic: When binary size reduction is required for releases or artifacts.

description: Pre-condition: Commit SHA provided. Transform: Polls GitHub API for check status. Post-condition: Succeeds if all checks passed, fails otherwise.
match_logic: When a step depends on the success of all other status checks.

description: Pre-condition: SARIF file exists. Transform: Filters out results based on inclusion/exclusion patterns. Post-condition: Modified SARIF file produced.
match_logic: When security scan results need to be cleaned of noise from specific paths.

description: Pre-condition: Issue labeled with support label. Transform: Adds comment and closes issue. Post-condition: Issue is closed with a comment.
side_effects: Modifies issue status and adds comments
match_logic: When automating the handling of support-related issues.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Downloads and installs Taskfile binary. Post-condition: `task` is available in PATH.
match_logic: When using Taskfile for build automation.

description: Pre-condition: Windows runner. Transform: Downloads and runs Windows SDK installer. Post-condition: Windows 10 SDK is installed.
match_logic: When building Windows applications requiring a specific SDK version.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Downloads and extracts ORAS binary. Post-condition: `oras` command is available in PATH.
match_logic: When working with OCI registries using ORAS.

description: Pre-condition: Nix installed. Transform: Authenticates and pushes Nix flakes to FlakeHub. Post-condition: Flake is published.
match_logic: When publishing Nix flakes to the FlakeHub registry.

description: Pre-condition: Git repo checked out. Transform: Updates version files or tags. Post-condition: Product version is updated.
match_logic: When automating versioning for HashiCorp-style products.

description: Pre-condition: ESPHome configuration file. Transform: Compiles firmware for target device. Post-condition: Firmware binary created.
match_logic: When building firmware for ESP8266/ESP32 using ESPHome.

description: Pre-condition: OpenShift credentials. Transform: Executes oc login and configures kubeconfig. Post-condition: `oc` and `kubectl` are authenticated.
match_logic: When deploying to or interacting with an OpenShift cluster.

description: Pre-condition: None. Transform: Detects CPU core count. Post-condition: Sets output with core count.
match_logic: When optimizing parallel tasks (e.g. make -j) based on runner capacity.

description: Pre-condition: x86_64 Linux runner. Transform: Boots a NetBSD VM using QEMU. Post-condition: Commands executed inside NetBSD environment.
match_logic: When testing software compatibility on NetBSD.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Downloads pre-built cargo-make binary. Post-condition: `cargo-make` is available in PATH.
match_logic: When using cargo-make for Rust project task automation.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials. Transform: Integrates application signals and telemetry. Post-condition: Observability data sent to AWS.
match_logic: When setting up AWS Application Signals for performance monitoring.

description: Pre-condition: Source files and target repo list. Transform: Pushes files to target repositories using Git. Post-condition: Files are updated in target repos.
match_logic: When keeping configurations or documentation consistent across multiple repos.

description: Pre-condition: k6 script and k6 installed. Transform: Executes k6 load tests. Post-condition: Test results produced.
match_logic: When performing load or performance testing with k6.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Downloads and installs NASM assembler. Post-condition: `nasm` available in PATH.
match_logic: When assembly compilation with NASM is required.

description: Pre-condition: Linux/Windows runner. Transform: Configures Xvfb or equivalent virtual display. Post-condition: DISPLAY environment variable set and active.
match_logic: When running GUI-based tests or visualization software in a headless CI environment.

description: Pre-condition: .editorconfig file present. Transform: Scans files for compliance with .editorconfig rules. Post-condition: Fails if violations found.
match_logic: When enforcing editorconfig standards across the codebase.

description: Pre-condition: Tag name and commit SHA. Transform: Updates the Git tag to point to the SHA. Post-condition: Tag points to new SHA.
side_effects: Modifies Git tags in remote repository
match_logic: When a rolling tag (like 'latest' or 'v1') needs to be updated.

description: Pre-condition: Depot credentials and Dockerfile. Transform: Offloads build to Depot managed builders. Post-condition: Image pushed to registry.
match_logic: When faster Docker builds via Depot's remote acceleration are desired.

description: Pre-condition: Linux/macOS runner. Transform: Installs the Determinate Nix distribution. Post-condition: Nix is installed and configured.
match_logic: When setting up a high-performance Nix environment.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Downloads docker-compose binary. Post-condition: `docker-compose` available in PATH.
match_logic: When a specific version of docker-compose is required.

description: Pre-condition: YAML files in PR. Transform: Runs yamllint and pipes output to reviewdog. Post-condition: Lint results posted as PR annotations.
match_logic: When linting YAML files and reporting via reviewdog annotations.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Installs the 'bob' tool. Post-condition: 'bob' is in PATH.
match_logic: When the 'bob' development tool is needed in the workflow.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Installs the 'enos' tool. Post-condition: 'enos' is in PATH.
match_logic: When the 'enos' environment orchestration tool is needed.

description: Pre-condition: Linear API token. Transform: Updates Linear issue status based on deployment. Post-condition: Linear release synced.
match_logic: When syncing GitHub deployments with Linear product management.

description: Pre-condition: PR event. Transform: Scans for secrets and pipes to reviewdog. Post-condition: Secrets reported as PR annotations.
match_logic: When scanning for accidental secret commits in PRs.

description: Pre-condition: Username and repo scope. Transform: Queries GitHub API for user roles. Post-condition: Sets output `check-result` (true/false).
match_logic: When restricting workflow steps based on the user's repository role.

description: Pre-condition: Linux runner (GPU preferred). Transform: Starts Ollama server and pulls models. Post-condition: Ollama API is available locally.
match_logic: When running LLM-based tasks locally on the runner.

description: Pre-condition: Command to run. Transform: Wraps command execution and registers problem matchers. Post-condition: Command output converted to GitHub annotations.
match_logic: When wrapping linter commands to ensure annotations work even for fork PRs.

description: Pre-condition: Issue created via issue form. Transform: Parses form values and applies labels. Post-condition: Issue is labeled.
match_logic: When automating labeling based on structured issue form data.

description: Pre-condition: dprint config present. Transform: Validates formatting of source files. Post-condition: Fails if files are not formatted.
match_logic: When using dprint to enforce code formatting.

description: Pre-condition: Dependency file. Transform: Finds latest versions and updates strings. Post-condition: Files updated with new versions.
match_logic: When automating the update of static dependency versions in Dockerfiles or scripts.

description: Pre-condition: Edge developer account and extension zip. Transform: Uploads and publishes to Edge Add-ons store. Post-condition: Extension published.
match_logic: When automating Edge extension releases.

description: Pre-condition: Swift package. Transform: Compiles for Android and executes tests in emulator. Post-condition: Test results produced.
match_logic: When cross-compiling and testing Swift code for Android.

description: Pre-condition: Markdown files. Transform: Scans for URLs and validates HTTP status. Post-condition: Fails if dead links found.
match_logic: When verifying documentation links.

description: Pre-condition: Slack Webhook/Token and job status. Transform: Sends formatted status message to Slack. Post-condition: Slack notification sent.
match_logic: When notifying Slack of workflow success or failure.

description: Pre-condition: Changes to commit. Transform: Uses bot GPG key to sign and push commit. Post-condition: Commit is verified on GitHub.
match_logic: When automated commits need to appear as 'Verified'.

description: Pre-condition: Cargo crate name. Transform: Restores cache or installs crate and saves cache. Post-condition: Crate binary is in PATH.
match_logic: When installing Rust tools via cargo and wanting to avoid re-compilation.

description: Pre-condition: Running on Blacksmith infrastructure. Transform: Configures persistent disk storage. Post-condition: Sticky disk mounted.
match_logic: When using Blacksmith runners and requiring persistent disk between runs.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access. Transform: Downloads and installs k6 binary. Post-condition: `k6` available in PATH.
match_logic: When preparing the environment for k6 load testing.

description: Pre-condition: Tailscale auth key. Transform: Starts Tailscale daemon and connects to tailnet. Post-condition: Runner is connected to Tailscale network.
match_logic: When secure access to private resources on a Tailscale network is needed.

description: Pre-condition: Resource URL or port. Transform: Polls the resource until it responds. Post-condition: Step completes when resource is ready.
match_logic: When a step depends on a service starting up (e.g. after docker-compose up).

description: Pre-condition: Windows runner. Transform: Installs Scoop package manager and requested apps. Post-condition: Apps available in PATH.
match_logic: When managing Windows dependencies using the Scoop package manager.

description: Pre-condition: A Docker image is available locally or in a registry. Transform: Pulls (if needed) and extracts specified files or directories from the Docker image to the runner's filesystem. Post-condition: The requested files are present on the runner's filesystem at the specified destination.
side_effects: Creates files on the runner's filesystem
match_logic: Use when you need to retrieve artifacts (like binaries or config files) that were built inside a Docker container without having to run the container.

description: Pre-condition: A pull request is being processed and a list of forbidden file patterns is provided. Transform: Compares the changed files in the PR against the forbidden list. Post-condition: Fails the workflow if any forbidden file was modified.
match_logic: Use to protect sensitive configuration files or critical code from being modified in pull requests by unauthorized contributors.

description: Pre-condition: Image files are present in the repository or specified path. Transform: Analyzes the images using a detection model for NSFW content. Post-condition: Reports findings and potentially fails the build if NSFW content is detected.
match_logic: Use to scan repository assets for inappropriate content to ensure compliance with community standards or safety policies.

description: Pre-condition: Stylelint output is expected in subsequent steps. Transform: Registers a regex-based problem matcher with the GitHub Actions runner. Post-condition: Stylelint errors and warnings in subsequent steps are automatically converted into GitHub workflow annotations.
side_effects: Modifies the runner's problem matcher configuration
match_logic: Use before running stylelint to see linting errors directly in the GitHub PR file diff view.

description: Pre-condition: Files exist on the runner and Sharepoint credentials (client ID/secret) are provided. Transform: Authenticates with Microsoft Graph/Sharepoint and uploads the specified files. Post-condition: The files are available in the target Sharepoint document library.
side_effects: Uploads data to an external service (Sharepoint)
match_logic: Use when you need to publish build artifacts or documentation to a corporate Sharepoint site.

description: Pre-condition: Binaries/files exist on the runner and DigiCert credentials/certificates are configured. Transform: Communicates with DigiCert Software Trust Manager to sign the provided files. Post-condition: The files are cryptographically signed and verified.
side_effects: Modifies files on disk by adding signatures
match_logic: Use for secure, automated code signing of releases for Windows, Linux, or macOS using DigiCert's enterprise platform.

description: Pre-condition: Values or expressions to be tested are provided as inputs. Transform: Evaluates the inputs against expected conditions. Post-condition: Fails the workflow if any assertion is false; otherwise succeeds.
match_logic: Use to validate that previous steps produced the expected outputs or that environment variables meet certain criteria before proceeding.

description: Pre-condition: Rust source code is present in the workspace and rustfmt is installed. Transform: Runs rustfmt --check on the project. Post-condition: Workflow fails if code is not formatted according to style guidelines; surfaces formatting errors as annotations.
match_logic: Use to enforce consistent Rust code style across a project automatically on pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: Twilio Account SID, Auth Token, and phone numbers are provided. Transform: Triggers a voice call via Twilio API with the specified message. Post-condition: A voice call is initiated to the target recipient.
match_logic: Use for urgent alerting or notifications that require direct voice communication upon workflow failure or critical event.

description: Pre-condition: Architecture-specific Docker images have been pushed to a registry. Transform: Creates and pushes a Docker manifest list (multi-arch image) pointing to the individual images. Post-condition: A single multi-architecture image tag is available in the registry.
side_effects: Creates and pushes a manifest list to a Docker registry
match_logic: Use after building images for different platforms (e.g., amd64, arm64) to combine them under a single multi-arch tag.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub Actions runner is active (Linux). Transform: Starts an OmniOS virtual machine and executes the provided scripts inside it. Post-condition: CI tasks are completed in an OmniOS environment and results are returned to the runner.
side_effects: Starts a virtual machine on the runner
match_logic: Use when you need to test code or build artifacts specifically for the OmniOS (illumos-based) operating system.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub repository and a GITHUB_TOKEN with appropriate permissions are available. Transform: Creates a new milestone in the repository with the provided title and description. Post-condition: A new milestone exists in the GitHub repository.
side_effects: Creates a new milestone in the GitHub repository
match_logic: Use to automate the creation of release milestones during a versioning or release workflow.

description: Pre-condition: FOSSA API key is provided and project dependencies are available. Transform: Scans project dependencies and uploads the results to FOSSA for license and vulnerability analysis. Post-condition: FOSSA analysis is completed and available in the FOSSA dashboard.
side_effects: Uploads dependency information to FOSSA service
match_logic: Use for automated open-source license compliance and security vulnerability scanning of project dependencies.

description: Pre-condition: The runner is active and internet access is available. Transform: Downloads and installs the specified version of the OpenHarmony SDK. Post-condition: The OpenHarmony SDK is installed and its location is added to the system PATH or provided as an output.
side_effects: Installs SDK files on the runner
match_logic: Use when building applications or libraries for the OpenHarmony operating system.

description: Pre-condition: A workflow is running. Transform: Instruments the runner to profile runtime behavior and evaluate security/performance assertions. Post-condition: Generates a profile report and fails the build if assertions are violated.
side_effects: Profiles system-level activity on the runner
match_logic: Use for advanced monitoring and security auditing of your GitHub Actions workflows at runtime.

description: Pre-condition: Files and directories exist in the workspace. Transform: Scans file and directory names against patterns defined in .ls-lint.yml. Post-condition: Workflow fails if any file or directory name violates the naming conventions.
match_logic: Use to enforce strict and consistent file and directory naming conventions across a codebase.

description: Pre-condition: Allure TestOps endpoint and token are provided. Transform: Downloads and configures the allurectl command-line tool. Post-condition: allurectl is available in the system PATH for uploading test results.
match_logic: Use when you need to integrate test results with Allure TestOps for advanced reporting.

description: Pre-condition: A tool or file needs to be cached between workflow runs. Transform: Uses the GitHub Actions tool-cache API to store or retrieve files based on a key. Post-condition: Specified tools are persisted and available across subsequent runs, speeding up setup.
side_effects: Modifies the runner's tool cache storage
match_logic: Use to cache custom binaries or tools that are not supported by standard caching actions to improve build speed.

description: Pre-condition: Depot project ID and API token are provided. Transform: Downloads and installs the Depot CLI and configures it for use on the runner. Post-condition: The depot command is available in the system PATH.
side_effects: Installs Depot CLI on the runner
match_logic: Use when you want to use Depot's remote build acceleration for Docker or other container builds.

description: Pre-condition: GitHub OIDC is configured and a NuGet repository URL is provided. Transform: Exchanges a GitHub OIDC token for a short-lived NuGet API key. Post-condition: A short-lived API key is available as an output for subsequent NuGet push operations.
side_effects: Requests an OIDC token from GitHub
match_logic: Use for secure, passwordless authentication to NuGet repositories during package publishing.

description: Pre-condition: A pull request or issue is opened by a contributor. Transform: Checks if this is the user's first contribution to the repository. Post-condition: Posts a predefined welcome comment if it is the user's first contribution.
side_effects: Posts a comment on a GitHub issue or pull request
match_logic: Use to build community engagement by automatically greeting and guiding new contributors to the project.

description: Pre-condition: A repository has one or more releases and a tag or latest keyword is provided. Transform: Queries the GitHub API for release metadata matching the criteria. Post-condition: Returns release details such as version, description, and asset URLs as outputs.
match_logic: Use when you need to programmatically retrieve metadata or download assets from a specific GitHub release.

description: Pre-condition: C++ source code is present and a GitHub token is provided. Transform: Runs cpplint on the source files and pipes the results to reviewdog. Post-condition: Code violations are reported as comments directly on the pull request.
side_effects: Creates comments on a GitHub pull request
match_logic: Use to enforce C++ coding standards (Google style) and surface violations as PR comments for easier review.

description: Pre-condition: AWS credentials are provided and an Inspector scan has been initiated or completed. Transform: Retrieves and analyzes the findings from AWS Inspector for the specified resource. Post-condition: Fails the workflow if vulnerabilities exceeding the defined severity threshold are found.
match_logic: Use to integrate AWS Inspector security scan results into your CI/CD pipeline to prevent deploying insecure infrastructure.

description: Pre-condition: A macOS binary/app exists and Apple Developer credentials (App-Specific Password, Team ID) are provided. Transform: Submits the software to Apple's Notarization Service and waits for approval. Post-condition: The software is notarized and can be distributed outside the Mac App Store.
side_effects: Uploads binaries to Apple's notarization service
match_logic: Use to automate the mandatory Apple notarization process for macOS software releases.

description: Pre-condition: Dependabot PRs exist and a cron schedule is defined. Transform: Checks if current time matches the schedule and merges eligible Dependabot PRs. Post-condition: Eligible dependency updates are merged into the base branch.
side_effects: Merges pull requests in the repository
match_logic: Use to reduce noise by batching and automatically merging Dependabot updates according to a specific schedule (e.g., weekly).

description: Pre-condition: Source code is present and ast-grep rules are defined. Transform: Executes structural code analysis using the ast-grep tool. Post-condition: Workflow fails if code violations are found based on the structural patterns.
match_logic: Use for complex linting tasks that require understanding code structure rather than just text patterns.

description: Pre-condition: Vim script files are present and a GitHub token is provided. Transform: Runs the vint linter on .vim files and reports results via reviewdog. Post-condition: Linting errors are posted as annotations or comments on the pull request.
side_effects: Creates comments on a GitHub pull request
match_logic: Use to maintain high-quality Vim script code by surfacing linting violations directly in the PR review.

description: Pre-condition: Telegram Bot Token, Channel ID, and files/messages are provided. Transform: Sends the specified content to a Telegram channel, utilizing session caching for efficiency. Post-condition: The message or file is posted to the target Telegram channel.
match_logic: Use to automatically notify a Telegram community or channel about build statuses, releases, or other repository events.

description: Pre-condition: A specific Packer version is requested. Transform: Downloads and installs the requested version of HashiCorp Packer on the runner. Post-condition: packer is available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use to ensure a consistent and specific version of HashiCorp Packer is used across all infrastructure-as-code workflows.

description: Pre-condition: Internet access is available on the runner. Transform: Installs the Microsoft Store Developer CLI tool. Post-condition: The msstore command is available in the system PATH.
side_effects: Installs Microsoft Store CLI on the runner
match_logic: Use when automating the submission or management of applications in the Microsoft Store.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub username and contribution history are accessible. Transform: Generates a SVG visualization of the user's contributions styled as a sakura garden. Post-condition: A decorative image file is generated and saved to the specified path.
side_effects: Generates and potentially commits a new image file
match_logic: Use to create a visually appealing, cherry-blossom-themed contribution graph for a GitHub profile README.

description: Pre-condition: A version of Packwiz is specified. Transform: Downloads and installs the Packwiz binary into the runner environment. Post-condition: The packwiz command is available for use in subsequent steps.
match_logic: Use when automating the creation, maintenance, or distribution of Minecraft modpacks using the Packwiz tool.

description: Pre-condition: A new pull request is opened and a welcome message is defined. Transform: Monitors for PR creation events and posts the welcome message. Post-condition: A welcome comment is visible on the newly created pull request.
side_effects: Posts a comment on a GitHub pull request
match_logic: Use to improve contributor experience by automatically providing guidance or thanks when a new PR is submitted.

description: Pre-condition: Flutter is vendored as a Git submodule in the repository. Transform: Configures the runner to use the version of Flutter contained within the submodule. Post-condition: The flutter command points to the submodule version and environment variables are set correctly.
side_effects: Modifies environment variables to point to vendored Flutter SDK
match_logic: Use for highly reproducible builds where the Flutter SDK is version-controlled as a project dependency rather than downloaded.

description: Pre-condition: A GitHub Issue or codebase problem is identified and an LLM API key is provided. Transform: Uses AI to analyze the context and propose or apply a code fix using the ledit tool. Post-condition: A solution is proposed via PR or the code is directly modified to resolve the issue.
side_effects: Creates PRs or comments using AI
match_logic: Use to experiment with autonomous AI-driven issue resolution and bug fixing within your repository.

description: Pre-condition: Template files with specific tags and a mapping of replacement values are provided. Transform: Iterates through files and replaces all instances of tags with their corresponding values. Post-condition: Files contain actual values instead of placeholders.
side_effects: Modifies files on the runner's filesystem
match_logic: Use for lightweight templating tasks, such as injecting build metadata into documentation or configuration files before deployment.

description: Pre-condition: The runner needs FFmpeg for media processing. Transform: Downloads and installs FFmpeg binaries for the runner's OS. Post-condition: ffmpeg and ffprobe are available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use whenever your workflow involves automated video transcoding, audio conversion, or media validation.

description: Pre-condition: An Rclone version is requested and cloud provider credentials are ready. Transform: Installs Rclone and optionally sets up the configuration file. Post-condition: rclone is available in the system PATH and ready for cloud sync operations.
match_logic: Use to automate file transfers, backups, or deployment artifact uploads to any of the 40+ cloud storage providers supported by Rclone.

description: Pre-condition: YouTube API credentials and video files are available. Transform: Installs the youtubeuploader CLI tool. Post-condition: The youtubeuploader command is available for uploading videos to YouTube.
match_logic: Use to automate the publishing of video content to a YouTube channel as part of a CI/CD or content pipeline.

description: Pre-condition: A Cloudflare Tunnel token is provided. Transform: Starts a Cloudflare Tunnel process (cloudflared) on the runner. Post-condition: A secure ingress tunnel is established, exposing the runner or a local port to a public URL.
side_effects: Exposes local ports to the internet
match_logic: Use when you need to provide public access to a web server or service running on the Actions runner for testing purposes.

description: Pre-condition: A Helm chart version and deployment context are provided. Transform: Checks against a defined policy or approval state for the specific chart version. Post-condition: The workflow proceeds only if the chart version is approved for the given deployment purpose.
match_logic: Use to implement custom approval gates and promotions for Helm-based Kubernetes deployments.

description: Pre-condition: A Svelte project exists in the workspace. Transform: Executes svelte-check to analyze Svelte files for type safety, a11y, and CSS issues. Post-condition: Workflow fails if critical errors are found; provides a summary of all warnings and errors.
match_logic: Use to ensure code quality and type correctness in Svelte projects as part of a CI pipeline.

description: Pre-condition: A .tool-versions file exists in the repository. Transform: Installs asdf and all plugins/tool versions specified in the .tool-versions file. Post-condition: All required language runtimes and CLI tools are installed and available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use in polyglot projects to manage and setup all tool versions from a single configuration file.

description: Pre-condition: A set of path filters (glob patterns) is provided. Transform: Compares the files changed in the current git context against the provided filters. Post-condition: Sets outputs indicating which filters matched, allowing for conditional step execution.
match_logic: Use to optimize monorepo CI workflows by only running jobs relevant to the specific directories or files that were modified.

description: Pre-condition: Azure credentials (via login action) and an ARM template file are provided. Transform: Validates and deploys the ARM template to an Azure Resource Group or Subscription. Post-condition: Azure resources are provisioned or updated to match the template definition.
match_logic: Use for Infrastructure-as-Code deployments on Azure using ARM templates.

description: Pre-condition: Files to be signed are present and Azure Artifact Signing service is accessible. Transform: Submits artifacts to the Azure signing service and applies the cryptographic signatures. Post-condition: Artifacts are signed with a trusted certificate.
side_effects: Modifies files on disk with signatures
match_logic: Use to ensure the integrity and authenticity of software artifacts before distribution using Azure's enterprise signing infrastructure.

description: Pre-condition: Azure credentials and Bicep template files are provided. Transform: Compiles (internally) and deploys the Bicep templates to Azure. Post-condition: Azure infrastructure is provisioned or updated according to the Bicep definitions.
match_logic: Use for modern Azure Infrastructure-as-Code deployments where Bicep is preferred over raw ARM templates.

description: Pre-condition: Azure credentials are provided and an Azure CLI script is defined. Transform: Executes the provided az commands within the GitHub Actions environment. Post-condition: Specified Azure management tasks are completed.
side_effects: Performs management operations on Azure resources
match_logic: Use for general-purpose Azure resource management tasks that are not covered by specialized deployment actions.

description: Pre-condition: Source code or a Docker image is available and Azure credentials are provided. Transform: Builds the container image (if needed) and deploys it to an Azure Container App environment. Post-condition: The specified Container App is running the new version of the code/image.
match_logic: Use to automate the deployment of containerized applications to Azure's managed serverless container service.

description: Pre-condition: Azure authentication active and project files present. Transform: Packages and uploads project files to Azure Functions. Post-condition: Function App is updated and running with new code.
match_logic: Use when deploying source code or a zip package to an Azure Function App.

description: Pre-condition: Azure authentication active and container image exists in a registry. Transform: Configures Azure Function App to pull and run the specified container image. Post-condition: Containerized Function App is updated with the new image.
side_effects: Updates Azure Function App container configuration
Triggers image pull and container restart
match_logic: Use when deploying a Docker container to an Azure Function App.

description: Pre-condition: Manifest templates (Helm/Kustomize) and value files present. Transform: Renders templates into static Kubernetes YAML manifests. Post-condition: Manifest files are available in the specified output directory.
side_effects: Generates local YAML files on the runner
match_logic: Use when you need to render Helm charts or Kustomize templates into plain Kubernetes YAML before deployment.

description: Pre-condition: Active Kubernetes context and secret data provided. Transform: Executes kubectl commands to create or update secrets in the cluster. Post-condition: Kubernetes secret exists in the target namespace.
side_effects: Creates or updates a secret in the Kubernetes cluster
match_logic: Use to provision Kubernetes secrets (generic, docker-registry, or tls) before deployment.

description: Pre-condition: Active Kubernetes context and manifest files available. Transform: Applies manifests to the cluster and monitors rollout status. Post-condition: Resources are deployed and confirmed healthy in the cluster.
side_effects: Modifies cluster state by deploying resources
match_logic: Use to deploy Kubernetes manifests to a cluster and track the rollout status.

description: Pre-condition: Kubernetes manifest files present. Transform: Validates manifest syntax and structure against schemas. Post-condition: Validation report generated; fails if errors found.
match_logic: Use to validate Kubernetes manifest files for syntax and schema correctness.

description: Pre-condition: Azure credentials or kubeconfig provided. Transform: Configures the runner's kubeconfig with target cluster details. Post-condition: Subsequent kubectl commands use the specified cluster context.
match_logic: Use as a prerequisite to set the Kubernetes context for other k8s actions or kubectl.

description: Pre-condition: Azure authentication and load test configuration present. Transform: Triggers and monitors a load test in Azure Load Testing service. Post-condition: Load test results are collected and analyzed against pass/fail criteria.
match_logic: Use to run automated performance and load tests in Azure.

description: Pre-condition: Valid Azure Service Principal or OIDC credentials provided as secrets. Transform: Authenticates the workflow runner with Azure. Post-condition: Azure environment variables and credentials configured for CLI and SDK use.
side_effects: Logs into Azure CLI and Azure PowerShell
match_logic: Use at the start of a workflow to authenticate with Azure for any subsequent Azure-related actions.

description: Pre-condition: Azure authentication (Azure/login) and PowerShell script provided. Transform: Executes the script within a pre-configured Azure PowerShell environment. Post-condition: Script completes and Azure resources are managed according to script logic.
side_effects: Executes PowerShell code that can modify Azure resources
match_logic: Use when you need to run Azure management tasks using PowerShell scripts.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment active. Transform: Downloads and installs the specified Helm CLI version. Post-condition: Helm binary is available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use to ensure a specific version of Helm is available for subsequent steps.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment active. Transform: Downloads and installs the specified kubectl CLI version. Post-condition: Kubectl binary is available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use to ensure a specific version of kubectl is available for subsequent steps.

description: Pre-condition: Azure authentication and SQL script/DACPAC files present. Transform: Connects to Azure SQL and applies the schema or data changes. Post-condition: Database schema or data is updated to match the input.
side_effects: Modifies Azure SQL database schema or data
match_logic: Use to automate database migrations or DACPAC deployments to Azure SQL.

description: Pre-condition: Azure authentication and build artifacts (code or container image) ready. Transform: Deploys the artifacts to an Azure Web App. Post-condition: Web App is running the new version of the application.
side_effects: Updates Azure Web App code or container
match_logic: Use to deploy web applications or containers to Azure App Service.

description: Pre-condition: Rust toolchain installed and crate name provided. Transform: Checks cache for binary; if missing, runs cargo install and updates cache. Post-condition: Crate binary is available in PATH.
side_effects: Installs binaries to cargo bin directory
match_logic: Use when you need to install a Rust-based tool and want to benefit from caching to speed up CI.

description: Pre-condition: Repository access provided via token. Transform: Creates and pushes empty commits to the repository. Post-condition: Contribution graph reflects new activity.
match_logic: Use to artificially maintain an active GitHub contribution graph.

description: Pre-condition: Target workflow run ID and attempt number provided. Transform: Locates and downloads the artifact associated with the specific run attempt. Post-condition: Artifact is extracted to the specified local path.
side_effects: Downloads and extracts files to the runner
match_logic: Use when you need to download artifacts from a specific historical run attempt instead of just the latest.

description: Pre-condition: Julia project and target workflow job name provided. Transform: Polls the GitHub API to check the status of the target job. Post-condition: Workflow proceeds only after the target job has completed successfully.
side_effects: Blocks workflow execution until job completion
match_logic: Use to synchronize job execution across different workflows based on Julia project dependencies.

description: Pre-condition: Running within a matrix job with outputs defined. Transform: Gathers outputs from all matrix instances into a single collection. Post-condition: Consolidated outputs are available for subsequent jobs.
match_logic: Use to collect and consolidate outputs from all shards of a matrix strategy.

description: Pre-condition: Target workflow filename and inputs provided. Transform: Sends a workflow_dispatch event via GitHub API to trigger the target workflow. Post-condition: Target workflow is queued or started.
match_logic: Use to chain workflows by manually triggering a downstream workflow with specific inputs.

description: Pre-condition: Triggered by gollum event and Slack webhook URL provided. Transform: Parses wiki change details and formats a message. Post-condition: Notification is sent to the specified Slack channel.
side_effects: Sends an external HTTP request to Slack
match_logic: Use to notify a Slack channel when a GitHub Wiki page is updated.

description: Pre-condition: Reusable workflow context. Transform: Executes defined composite steps. Post-condition: Logic encapsulated in the action is completed.
match_logic: Use as a template or building block for custom composite actions (generic usage).

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment active. Transform: Downloads and installs the Biome CLI. Post-condition: Biome is available in the system PATH.
match_logic: Use to set up the Biome toolchain for linting and formatting JavaScript/TypeScript code.

description: Pre-condition: Git repository with conventional commits present. Transform: Analyzes git history to calculate the next semantic version. Post-condition: New version string is generated and available as an output.
match_logic: Use to automatically calculate the next semantic version based on conventional commit messages.

description: Pre-condition: Bitwarden access token or credentials provided. Transform: Fetches requested secrets from Bitwarden Secrets Manager or Vault. Post-condition: Secrets are exported as environment variables or available as outputs.
match_logic: Use to securely retrieve and inject secrets from Bitwarden into your workflow.

description: Pre-condition: Metric data (coverage/tests) and repository write access available. Transform: Generates badge SVGs and commits them to the repository. Post-condition: Updated badges are visible in the repository.
side_effects: Commits and pushes to the repository
match_logic: Use to automatically generate and commit status badges to your repository's README.

description: Pre-condition: Apple certificates and provisioning profiles provided as secrets. Transform: Signs the application binary and submits it for notarization. Post-condition: Binary is signed and notarized by Apple.
match_logic: Use to automate Apple code signing and notarization for macOS or iOS apps.

description: Pre-condition: Dart/Flutter environment and melos.yaml present. Transform: Installs and initializes Melos monorepo manager. Post-condition: Melos is available for running cross-package commands.
match_logic: Use to manage Dart or Flutter monorepos using Melos in CI.

description: Pre-condition: Node.js environment and package.json present. Transform: Executes specified Yarn commands (e.g. install, build). Post-condition: Yarn tasks completed successfully.
side_effects: Runs scripts that can modify the file system
match_logic: Use as a wrapper to run Yarn commands in a structured GitHub Action step.

description: Pre-condition: Infrastructure-as-code files (Terraform/K8s/etc.) present. Transform: Scans files for security and compliance misconfigurations. Post-condition: Scan report generated; fails if policy violations detected.
side_effects: Can upload SARIF to GitHub Security tab
match_logic: Use to perform static security analysis (IaC scanning) on cloud configuration files.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment active. Transform: Downloads and configures the requested Chrome/Chromium version. Post-condition: Chrome binary is available for browser testing.
side_effects: Installs browser binaries on the runner
match_logic: Use to install a specific version of Chrome for automated browser testing.

description: Pre-condition: Runner environment active. Transform: Downloads and configures the requested Firefox version. Post-condition: Firefox binary is available for browser testing.
side_effects: Installs browser binaries on the runner
match_logic: Use to install a specific version of Firefox for automated browser testing.

description: Pre-condition: Protobuf files and buf.yaml present. Transform: Runs linting, formatting, or breaking change detection. Post-condition: API contract validated; optionally published to Buf Registry.
side_effects: May publish to Buf Schema Registry
match_logic: Use to enforce Protobuf standards and detect breaking changes in gRPC/API projects.

description: Pre-condition: Test result XML/JSON files generated in previous step. Transform: Uploads test data to BuildPulse for analysis. Post-condition: Flaky tests identified and tracked in BuildPulse dashboard.
side_effects: Uploads test data to an external service
match_logic: Use to monitor and analyze flaky tests across your CI pipeline.

description: Pre-condition: API specification file (OpenAPI/AsyncAPI) and Bump.sh token provided. Transform: Validates and deploys the specification to Bump.sh. Post-condition: Documentation portal is updated with the new spec version.
side_effects: Deploys documentation to an external service
match_logic: Use to automatically update API documentation on Bump.sh from your repository.

description: Pre-condition: SSH private key and destination server details provided. Transform: Synchronizes local files to a remote server using rsync over SSH. Post-condition: Remote server files match local files incrementally.
side_effects: Modifies files on a remote server via SSH
match_logic: Use for incremental file deployment to traditional servers over SSH.

description: Pre-condition: Gradle project present. Transform: Restores and saves Gradle dependency and build caches. Post-condition: Gradle build performance improved in subsequent runs.
side_effects: Manages GitHub Actions cache for Gradle
match_logic: Use to speed up Gradle-based Java/Android builds by using specialized caching.

description: Pre-condition: WebAssembly project and requested tool (e.g. Wasmtime) provided. Transform: Sets up the Bytecode Alliance toolchain on the runner. Post-condition: Wasm tools are available in the PATH.
match_logic: Use to install and configure WebAssembly tools like Wasmtime or WASI.

description: Pre-condition: Pull requests exist in the repository. Transform: Identifies stale PRs and adds helpful comments or labels. Post-condition: PR queue is managed and contributors are prompted for updates.
side_effects: Adds comments and labels to pull requests
match_logic: Use to automate the management and follow-up of stale pull requests.

description: Pre-condition: Expired or redundant artifacts exist in the repository. Transform: Deletes artifacts based on age or name patterns. Post-condition: Repository artifact storage is cleaned up and quota is recovered.
side_effects: Permanently deletes workflow artifacts from GitHub
match_logic: Use to manage and cleanup GitHub Actions artifact storage.

description: Pre-condition: Nix installed and Cachix cache name provided. Transform: Configures Nix to use the specified Cachix binary cache. Post-condition: Nix builds are faster as results are pulled from/pushed to the cache.
side_effects: Uploads/downloads nix store paths to/from Cachix
match_logic: Use to speed up Nix builds by sharing binary artifacts via Cachix.
